   621	    Find the center coordinate (plot) of the GOI.
   622	    If multiple GOIs, average them. If none, usage center of the range.
   623	    """
   624	    goi_centers = []
   625	    for g in genes:
   626	        # Check 'name' and 'home_gene_id'
   627	        if is_goi(g.get("name")) or is_goi(g.get("home_gene_id")):
   628	            goi_centers.append((g["start_plot"] + g["end_plot"]) / 2)
   629	            
   630	    if goi_centers:
   631	        return sum(goi_centers) / len(goi_centers)
   632	        
   633	    # Fallback: center of the entire cluster
   634	    if not genes:
   635	        return 0
   636	    start = min(g["start_plot"] for g in genes)
   637	    end   = max(g["end_plot"]   for g in genes)
   638	    return (start + end) / 2
   639	
   640	def _goi_colour_for_genome(genome_id, goi_genome_colours):
   641	    """Look up GOI colour for a specific genome, with fuzzy matching."""
   642	    if not goi_genome_colours:
   643	        return GOI_COLOUR
   644	    # Exact match
   645	    if genome_id in goi_genome_colours:
   646	        return goi_genome_colours[genome_id]
   647	    # Prefix match  (e.g. "GCF_029169275.1" in "GCF_029169275.1.fna")
   648	    for key, clr in goi_genome_colours.items():
   649	        if key in genome_id or genome_id in key:
   650	            return clr
   651	    # Home fallback
   652	    return goi_genome_colours.get("home", GOI_COLOUR)
   653	
   654	
   655	def _lookup_product(gene_name, products):
   656	    """Fuzzy product-name lookup."""
   657	    for candidate in (gene_name, gene_name.replace("gene-", ""),
   658	                      "gene-" + gene_name if not gene_name.startswith("gene-") else ""):
   659	        if candidate in products:
   660	            return products[candidate]
   661	    return ""
   662	
   663	
   664	# ======================================================================
   665	# Tree visualization
   666	# ======================================================================
   667	
   668	def _render_tree_html(tree_file, goi_genome_colours, output_path,
   669	                      species_map=None):
   670	    """
   671	    Render a horizontal dendrogram of the GOI phylogenetic tree as an
   672	    interactive Plotly HTML file.  Leaf nodes are coloured with the same
   673	    clade palette used in the synteny plot.
   674	    """
   675	    if not tree_file or not os.path.exists(tree_file) or not ETE3_AVAILABLE:
   676	        return
   677	
   678	    try:
   679	        t = Tree(tree_file)
   680	    except Exception as exc:
   681	        print(f"Warning: could not parse tree for rendering: {exc}")
   682	        return
   683	
   684	    leaves = list(t.iter_leaves())
   685	    if len(leaves) < 2:
   686	        return
   687	
   688	    # --- 1. Assign (x, y) coordinates via recursive DFS ----------------
   689	    # x = branch length (horizontal), y = leaf index (vertical)
   690	    node_coords = {}           # node -> (x, y)
   691	    leaf_counter = [0]         # mutable counter
   692	
   693	    def _layout(node, x_offset):
   694	        if node.is_leaf():
   695	            y = leaf_counter[0]
   696	            leaf_counter[0] += 1
   697	            node_coords[node] = (x_offset + node.dist, y)
   698	        else:
   699	            child_ys = []
   700	            for child in node.children:
   701	                _layout(child, x_offset + node.dist)
   702	                child_ys.append(node_coords[child][1])
   703	            node_coords[node] = (x_offset + node.dist, sum(child_ys) / len(child_ys))
   704	
   705	    _layout(t, 0)
   706	
   707	    # --- 2. Build Plotly traces ----------------------------------------
   708	    fig = go.Figure()
   709	
   710	    # Branch lines (parent -> child: horizontal then vertical)
   711	    for node in t.traverse():
   712	        if node.is_root():
   713	            continue
   714	        parent = node.up
   715	        px, py = node_coords[parent]
   716	        cx, cy = node_coords[node]
   717	        # Horizontal line from parent x to child x, at child y
   718	        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
   719	            x=[px, px, cx], y=[py, cy, cy],
   720	            mode="lines",
   721	            line=dict(color="black", width=1.5),
   722	            hoverinfo="skip", showlegend=False,
   723	        ))
   724	
   725	    # Leaf dots + labels
   726	    for leaf in leaves:
   727	        lx, ly = node_coords[leaf]
   728	        gid = _genome_id_from_leaf(leaf.name)
   729	        key = gid if gid else "home"
   730	
   731	        # Colour: try exact, then fuzzy
   732	        colour = GOI_COLOUR
   733	        if goi_genome_colours:
   734	            if key in goi_genome_colours:
   735	                colour = goi_genome_colours[key]
   736	            else:
   737	                for k, c in goi_genome_colours.items():
   738	                    if k in key or key in k:
   739	                        colour = c
   740	                        break
   741	
   742	        # Clean label
   743	        label = leaf.name
   744	        if "|" in label:
   745	            parts = label.split("|")
   746	            goi_part = parts[0]
   747	            genome_part = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""
   748	            # Prettify genome ID — use species name when available
   749	            genome_pretty = genome_part.replace("_fna_exon_ann", "").replace("_fna", "")
   750	            if species_map:
   751	                for acc, sp_name in species_map.items():
   752	                    if acc in genome_pretty:
   753	                        genome_pretty = sp_name
   754	                        break
   755	            label = f"{goi_part} | {genome_pretty}"
   756	        else:
   757	            label = f"{label} (home)"
   758	
   759	        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
   760	            x=[lx], y=[ly],
   761	            mode="markers+text",
   762	            marker=dict(size=14, color=colour, line=dict(color="black", width=1)),
   763	            text=[label],
   764	            textposition="middle right",
   765	            textfont=dict(size=11),
   766	            hovertext=f"<b>{leaf.name}</b><br>Branch length: {leaf.dist:.6f}",
   767	            hoverinfo="text",
   768	            showlegend=False,
   769	        ))
   770	
   771	    # --- 3. Layout -----------------------------------------------------
   772	    n_leaves = len(leaves)
   773	    fig.update_layout(
   774	        title=dict(
   775	            text="<b>SynTerra GOI Phylogenetic Tree</b>",
   776	            x=0.5, font=dict(size=15),
   777	        ),
   778	        height=max(300, n_leaves * 60 + 100),
   779	        width=900,
   780	        xaxis=dict(
   781	            title="Evolutionary distance",
   782	            showgrid=True, gridcolor="rgba(200,200,200,0.3)",
   783	            zeroline=True,
   784	        ),
   785	        yaxis=dict(
   786	            showticklabels=False, showgrid=False, zeroline=False,
   787	            range=[-0.5, n_leaves - 0.5],
   788	        ),
   789	        plot_bgcolor="white",
   790	        paper_bgcolor="white",
   791	        margin=dict(l=40, r=300, t=60, b=50),
   792	    )
   793	
   794	    fig.write_html(output_path)
   795	    print(f"Tree plot saved to {output_path}")
   796	
   797	
   798	# ======================================================================
   799	# Main
   800	# ======================================================================
   801	
   802	def main():
   803	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="SynTerra synteny plot")
   804	    ap.add_argument("--home_bed",       required=True)
   805	    ap.add_argument("--home_gff",       default=None)
   806	    ap.add_argument("--query_bed",      default=None)
   807	    ap.add_argument("--target_gffs",    nargs="*", default=[])
   808	    ap.add_argument("--target_names",   nargs="*", default=[])
   809	    ap.add_argument("--candidate_beds", nargs="*", default=[])
   810	    ap.add_argument("--homology_tsvs",  nargs="*", default=[])
   811	    ap.add_argument("--tree",           default=None)
   812	    ap.add_argument("--species_map",    default=None,
   813	                    help="TSV mapping accession → species name")
   814	    ap.add_argument("--output",         required=True)
   815	    args = ap.parse_args()
   816	
   817	    # -- 0. Load species mapping -----------------------------------------
   818	    species_map = {}  # accession -> species name
   819	    if args.species_map and os.path.exists(args.species_map):
   820	        with open(args.species_map) as fh:
   821	            for line in fh:
   822	                parts = line.strip().split('\t')
   823	                if len(parts) >= 2:
   824	                    # Format can be:
   825	                    # 2 columns: accession<TAB>species
   826	                    # 3 columns: accession<TAB>species<TAB>tax_level
   827	                    species_map[parts[0]] = parts[1]
   828	        print(f"[plot] Loaded species mapping for {len(species_map)} genomes")
   829	
   830	    # -- 1. Parse inputs -------------------------------------------------
   831	
   832	    home_genes = parse_bed(args.home_bed)
   833	    if not home_genes:
   834	        msg = f"ERROR: empty home BED: {args.home_bed}"
   835	        print(msg, file=sys.stderr)
   836	        fig = go.Figure()
   837	        fig.add_annotation(
   838	            text=msg,
   839	            x=0.5, y=0.5, xref="paper", yref="paper",
   840	            showarrow=False,
   841	            font=dict(size=14, color="crimson"),
   842	        )
   843	        fig.update_layout(
   844	            title="SynTerra Synteny Plot (Failed: empty home BED)",
   845	            plot_bgcolor="white",
   846	            paper_bgcolor="white",
   847	        )
   848	        fig.write_html(args.output)
   849	        sys.exit(2)
   850	    home_genes.sort(key=lambda g: g["start"])
   851	
   852	    query_intervals = []
   853	    if args.query_bed and os.path.exists(args.query_bed):
   854	        for g in parse_bed(args.query_bed):
   855	            query_intervals.append({"chrom": g["chrom"],
   856	                                    "start": g["start"], "end": g["end"]})
   857	
   858	    # Identify GOI gene names dynamically from query_bed overlap
   859	    identify_goi_names(home_genes, query_intervals)
   860	
   861	    home_products = parse_home_gff_products(args.home_gff) if args.home_gff else {}
   862	    homology_map  = parse_homology_tsvs(args.homology_tsvs)
   863	
   864	    goi_genome_colours, tree_target_order = parse_tree_clade_colours(args.tree)
   865	
   866	    # -- 2. Build target tracks (matched by filename, not positional index)
   867	    candidate_regions_by_genome = parse_candidate_regions(args.candidate_beds)
   868	
   869	    target_tracks = []
   870	    for gff_file in args.target_gffs:
   871	        genome_id = clean_genome_name(
   872	            os.path.basename(gff_file).replace(".gff", ""))
   873	        genes = parse_target_gff(gff_file)
   874	        genes = filter_genes_to_candidate_regions(
   875	            genes,
   876	            _match_regions_for_genome(candidate_regions_by_genome, genome_id),
   877	        )
   878	        if not genes:
   879	            continue
   880	        genes.sort(key=lambda g: g["start"])
   881	        # Use species name from mapping if available
   882	        display = genome_id
   883	        for acc, sp_name in species_map.items():
   884	            if acc in genome_id:
   885	                display = sp_name
   886	                break
   887	        target_tracks.append({
   888	            "genome_id":    genome_id,
   889	            "display_name": display,
   890	            "genes":        genes,
   891	            "chrom":        genes[0]["chrom"],
   892	        })
   893	
   894	    # Order targets by phylogenetic distance (if tree available), else alphabetically by species name
   895	    if tree_target_order:
   896	        def _tree_key(t):
   897	            for i, gid in enumerate(tree_target_order):
   898	                if gid in t["genome_id"]:
   899	                    return i
   900	            return 999
   901	        target_tracks.sort(key=_tree_key)
   902	    else:
   903	        # Fallback: order by display name (species name) alphabetically
   904	        target_tracks.sort(key=lambda t: t["display_name"])
   905	
   906	    # -- 3. Colour map ---------------------------------------------------
   907	
   908	    gene_colours = assign_gene_colours(home_genes, query_intervals)
   909	
   910	    # -- 4. Assemble track list & Compress -------------------------------
   911	
   912	    home_chrom = home_genes[0]["chrom"]
   913	    
   914	    # Initial list with raw genes
   915	    raw_tracks = [{
   916	        "label":     f"Home genome ({home_chrom})",
   917	        "genes":     home_genes,
   918	        "is_home":   True,
   919	        "genome_id": "home",
   920	        "chrom":     home_chrom
   921	    }]
   922	    for tt in target_tracks:
   923	        raw_tracks.append({
   924	            "label":     f"{tt['display_name']} ({tt['chrom']})",
   925	            "genes":     tt["genes"],
   926	            "is_home":   False,
   927	            "genome_id": tt["genome_id"],
   928	            "chrom":     tt["chrom"]
   929	        })
   930	
   931	    all_tracks = []
   932	    for track in raw_tracks:
   933	        # 1. Compress
   934	        c_genes, breaks = compress_track_coordinates(track["genes"], threshold=50000, visual_gap=3000)
   935	        track["genes"]  = c_genes
   936	        track["breaks"] = breaks
   937	        
   938	        # 2. Find Anchor (GOI center) to align at x=0
   939	        anchor = get_anchor_center(c_genes)
   940	        track["offset"] = anchor  # This effectively centers the plot on the GOI
   941	        
   942	        all_tracks.append(track)
   943	
   944	    n_tracks = len(all_tracks)
   945	
   946	    # -- 5. Layout geometry -----------------------------------------------
   947	
   948	    GENE_H      = 0.35          # gene arrow height
   949	    TRACK_SPACE = 1.4           # vertical pitch between tracks
   950	    RIBBON_GAP  = 0.12          # gap between gene arrow and ribbon edge
   951	
   952	    fig = go.Figure()
   953	
   954	    # -- 5a. Track background bands --------------------------------------
   955	    for ti, track in enumerate(all_tracks):
   956	        yb    = (n_tracks - 1 - ti) * TRACK_SPACE
   957	        x_off = track["offset"]
   958	        if not track["genes"]:
   959	            continue
   960	        # Use plotted coordinates for background extent
   961	        x_min = min(g["start_plot"] for g in track["genes"]) - x_off - 1000
   962	        x_max = max(g["end_plot"]   for g in track["genes"]) - x_off + 1000
   963	        fig.add_shape(
   964	            type="rect",
   965	            x0=x_min, x1=x_max, y0=yb - 0.02, y1=yb + GENE_H + 0.02,
   966	            fillcolor=TRACK_BG_CLR, line=dict(width=0), layer="below",
   967	        )
   968	
   969	    # -- 5b. Ribbons (draw first so they sit behind genes) ---------------
   970	    for ti in range(n_tracks - 1):
   971	        upper = all_tracks[ti]
   972	        lower = all_tracks[ti + 1]
   973	        y_u = (n_tracks - 1 - ti)       * TRACK_SPACE
   974	        y_l = (n_tracks - 1 - (ti + 1)) * TRACK_SPACE
   975	        y_ribbon_top = y_u - RIBBON_GAP
   976	        y_ribbon_bot = y_l + GENE_H + RIBBON_GAP
   977	
   978	        for lg in lower["genes"]:
   979	            home_id = lg.get("home_gene_id", "")
   980	            if not home_id:
   981	                continue
   982	            for ug in upper["genes"]:
   983	                u_name = ug["name"]
   984	                u_home = ug.get("home_gene_id", u_name)
   985	                match = (u_home == home_id or u_name == home_id
   986	                         or (is_goi(u_home) and is_goi(home_id))
   987	                         or (is_goi(u_name) and is_goi(home_id)))
   988	                if match:
   989	                    # Determine ribbon colour
   990	                    colour = gene_colours.get(home_id,
   991	                             gene_colours.get(u_name, UNMATCHED_CLR))
   992	                    if is_goi(home_id):
   993	                        colour = _goi_colour_for_genome(
   994	                            lower["genome_id"], goi_genome_colours)
   995	                    add_ribbon(fig, ug, lg,
   996	                               upper["offset"], lower["offset"],
   997	                               y_ribbon_top, y_ribbon_bot,
   998	                               colour, alpha=0.20)
   999	                    break
  1000	
  1001	    # -- 5c. Gene arrows -------------------------------------------------
  1002	    legend_shown = set()
  1003	
  1004	    for ti, track in enumerate(all_tracks):
  1005	        yb    = (n_tracks - 1 - ti) * TRACK_SPACE
  1006	        x_off = track["offset"]
  1007	
  1008	        # Draw large genes first so small genes render on top
  1009	        # Use plot coordinates for size sorting? Yes.
  1010	        sorted_genes = sorted(track["genes"],
  1011	                               key=lambda g: g["end_plot"] - g["start_plot"],
  1012	                               reverse=True)
  1013	
  1014	        for gene in sorted_genes:
  1015	            name    = gene["name"]
  1016	            home_id = gene.get("home_gene_id", name)
  1017	            goi_f   = is_goi(name) or is_goi(home_id)
  1018	
  1019	            # --- colour ---
  1020	            if goi_f:
  1021	                colour = _goi_colour_for_genome(
  1022	                    track["genome_id"], goi_genome_colours)
  1023	                bclr, bw = GOI_BORDER, 2.5
  1024	            elif home_id in gene_colours:
  1025	                colour = gene_colours[home_id]
  1026	                bclr, bw = "rgba(0,0,0,0.35)", 1
  1027	            elif name in gene_colours:
  1028	                colour = gene_colours[name]
  1029	                bclr, bw = "rgba(0,0,0,0.35)", 1
  1030	            else:
  1031	                colour = UNMATCHED_CLR
  1032	                bclr, bw = "rgba(0,0,0,0.15)", 0.5
  1033	
  1034	            # --- hover text ---
  1035	            cn = clean_gene_label(name)
  1036	            if track["is_home"]:
  1037	                product = _lookup_product(name, home_products)
  1038	                hover = f"<b>{cn}</b>"
  1039	                if product:
  1040	                    hover += f"<br><i>{product}</i>"
  1041	                # Use raw coords for hover
  1042	                hover += (f"<br>{gene['chrom']}:{gene['start']:,}-{gene['end']:,}"
  1043	                          f"<br>Strand: {gene['strand']}")
  1044	                if goi_f:
  1045	                    hover += "<br><b>GENE OF INTEREST</b>"
  1046	            else:
  1047	                hover = f"<b>{cn}</b>"
  1048	                hover += f"<br>Homolog: {clean_gene_label(home_id)}"
  1049	                if "identity" in gene:
  1050	                    hover += f"<br>Identity: {gene['identity']:.1f}%"
  1051	                if "n_exons" in gene:
  1052	                    hover += f"<br>Exons: {gene['n_exons']}"
  1053	                hover += (f"<br>{gene['chrom']}:{gene['start']:,}-{gene['end']:,}"
  1054	                          f"<br>Strand: {gene['strand']}")
  1055	                if goi_f:
  1056	                    hover += "<br><b>GENE OF INTEREST</b>"
  1057	
  1058	            # --- legend (one entry per home-gene name) ---
  1059	            lg_key = home_id if home_id else name
  1060	            show_leg = lg_key not in legend_shown
  1061	            if show_leg:
  1062	                legend_shown.add(lg_key)
  1063	
  1064	            add_gene(fig, gene, x_off, yb, GENE_H, colour, bclr, bw,
  1065	                     hover, show_leg, lg_key)
  1066	
  1067	    # -- 5d. Gene labels -------------------------------------------------
  1068	    for ti, track in enumerate(all_tracks):
  1069	        yb    = (n_tracks - 1 - ti) * TRACK_SPACE
  1070	        x_off = track["offset"]
  1071	        for gene in track["genes"]:
  1072	            name    = gene["name"]
  1073	            home_id = gene.get("home_gene_id", name)
  1074	            goi_f   = is_goi(name) or is_goi(home_id)
  1075	            if not track["is_home"] and home_id:
  1076	                label = clean_gene_label(home_id)
  1077	            else:
  1078	                label = clean_gene_label(name)
  1079	            add_label(fig, gene, x_off, yb, GENE_H, label,
  1080	                      fsize=8, is_goi_flag=goi_f)
  1081	
  1082	    # -- 5e. Track labels (left margin) ----------------------------------
  1083	    for ti, track in enumerate(all_tracks):
  1084	        yb = (n_tracks - 1 - ti) * TRACK_SPACE
  1085	        fig.add_annotation(
  1086	            x=-0.01, y=yb + GENE_H / 2,
  1087	            text=f"<b>{track['label']}</b>",
  1088	            showarrow=False,
  1089	            font=dict(size=11, color="black"),
  1090	            xref="paper", yref="y",
  1091	            xanchor="right", yanchor="middle",
  1092	        )
  1093	
  1094	    # -- 5f. Gap breaks --------------------------------------------------
  1095	    for ti, track in enumerate(all_tracks):
  1096	        yb    = (n_tracks - 1 - ti) * TRACK_SPACE
  1097	        x_off = track["offset"]
  1098	        for brk in track.get("breaks", []):
  1099	            draw_gap_break(fig, brk["x"] - x_off, yb, GENE_H, brk["text"])
  1100	
  1101	    # -- 6. Figure styling -----------------------------------------------
  1102	
  1103	    # Compute plotted range
  1104	    # Collect all plotted X coordinates
  1105	    all_x = []
  1106	    for track in all_tracks:
  1107	        x_off = track["offset"]
  1108	        for g in track["genes"]:
  1109	            all_x.append(g["start_plot"] - x_off)
  1110	            all_x.append(g["end_plot"]   - x_off)
  1111	
  1112	    if not all_x:
  1113	        x_min, x_max = -1000, 1000
  1114	    else:
  1115	        x_min, x_max = min(all_x), max(all_x)
  1116	        
  1117	    pad = (x_max - x_min) * 0.05 + 5000
  1118	    x_range = [x_min - pad, x_max + pad]
  1119	
  1120	    fig_height = max(500, n_tracks * 200 + 80)
  1121	
  1122	    fig.update_layout(
  1123	        title=dict(
  1124	            text=("<b>SynTerra Synteny Plot</b>"
  1125	                  "<br><sup>Genes coloured by homology group | "
  1126	                  "* = Gene of Interest | Ribbons connect orthologs | // = Compressed Gaps</sup>"),
  1127	            x=0.5, font=dict(size=15),
  1128	        ),
  1129	        height=fig_height,
  1130	        width=1500,
  1131	        xaxis=dict(
  1132	            title="", # No title since numbers are relative/discontinuous
  1133	            showgrid=False, 
  1134	            zeroline=False,
  1135	            showticklabels=False, # Hide ticks as they are discontinuous
  1136	            range=x_range,
  1137	        ),
  1138	        yaxis=dict(
  1139	            showticklabels=False, showgrid=False, zeroline=False,
  1140	            range=[-0.6,
  1141	                   (n_tracks - 1) * TRACK_SPACE + GENE_H + 1.0],
  1142	        ),
  1143	        plot_bgcolor="white",
  1144	        paper_bgcolor="white",
  1145	        margin=dict(l=260, r=60, t=85, b=55), # Increased left margin
  1146	        legend=dict(
  1147	            title="<b>Gene (home ID)</b>",
  1148	            orientation="v", x=1.01, y=1.0,
  1149	            font=dict(size=9),
  1150	            tracegroupgap=2,
  1151	        ),
  1152	        hovermode="closest",
  1153	    )
  1154	    
  1155	    # Add Scale Bar (10 kb)
  1156	    # Place it in bottom right? Or top left?
  1157	    # Let's put it at bottom right
  1158	    scale_len = 10000
  1159	    sb_x1 = x_max
  1160	    sb_x0 = x_max - scale_len
  1161	    sb_y  = -0.4
  1162	    
  1163	    fig.add_shape(
  1164	        type="line",
  1165	        x0=sb_x0, x1=sb_x1, y0=sb_y, y1=sb_y,
  1166	        line=dict(color="black", width=3),
  1167	    )
  1168	    fig.add_annotation(
  1169	        x=(sb_x0 + sb_x1)/2, y=sb_y - 0.1,
  1170	        text="<b>10 kb</b>",
  1171	        showarrow=False,
  1172	        font=dict(size=10, color="black"),
  1173	        yanchor="top"
  1174	    )
  1175	
  1176	    fig.write_html(args.output)
  1177	    print(f"Synteny plot saved to {args.output}")
  1178	    print(f"  Tracks: {n_tracks} (1 home + {len(target_tracks)} targets)")
  1179	    print(f"  Gap compression: active (>50kb -> 3kb visual)")
  1180	
  1181	    # -- 7. Tree plot (separate HTML) ------------------------------------
  1182	    tree_output = args.output.replace("_synteny_plot.html", "_tree.html")
  1183	    if tree_output == args.output:
  1184	        tree_output = args.output.replace(".html", "_tree.html")
  1185	    _render_tree_html(args.tree, goi_genome_colours, tree_output,
  1186	                      species_map=species_map)
  1187	
  1188	
  1189	if __name__ == "__main__":
  1190	    main()


===== ./bin/prodigal_on_regions.py =====
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	
     3	import argparse
     4	import os
     5	import shutil
     6	import subprocess
     7	import sys
     8	import tempfile
     9	
    10	try:
    11	    from sequence_utils import load_genome, write_fasta, parse_fasta
    12	except ImportError:
    13	    sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
    14	    from sequence_utils import load_genome, write_fasta, parse_fasta
    15	
    16	
    17	def str2bool(value: str) -> bool:
    18	    if isinstance(value, bool):
    19	        return value
    20	    if value is None:
    21	        return False
    22	    val = value.strip().lower()
    23	    if val in {"true", "1", "yes", "y", "t"}:
    24	        return True
    25	    if val in {"false", "0", "no", "n", "f"}:
    26	        return False
    27	    raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"Invalid boolean value: {value}")
    28	
    29	
    30	def parse_bed(path):
    31	    regions = []
    32	    if not path or not os.path.exists(path) or os.path.getsize(path) == 0:
    33	        return regions
    34	    with open(path, "r") as f:
    35	        for line in f:
    36	            if not line.strip() or line.startswith("#"):
    37	                continue
    38	            parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
    39	            if len(parts) < 3:
    40	                continue
    41	            try:
    42	                chrom = parts[0]
    43	                start = int(parts[1])
    44	                end = int(parts[2])
    45	                if end > start:
    46	                    regions.append((chrom, start, end))
    47	            except ValueError:
    48	                continue
    49	    return regions
    50	
    51	
    52	def merge_regions(regions, genome_seqs, window):
    53	    by_chrom = {}
    54	    for chrom, start, end in regions:
    55	        if chrom not in genome_seqs:
    56	            continue
    57	        slen = len(genome_seqs[chrom])
    58	        w_start = max(0, start - window)
    59	        w_end = min(slen, end + window)
    60	        if w_end <= w_start:
    61	            continue
    62	        by_chrom.setdefault(chrom, []).append((w_start, w_end))
    63	
    64	    merged = []
    65	    for chrom, spans in by_chrom.items():
    66	        spans.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
    67	        cur_start, cur_end = spans[0]
    68	        for s, e in spans[1:]:
    69	            if s <= cur_end:
    70	                cur_end = max(cur_end, e)
    71	            else:
    72	                merged.append((chrom, cur_start, cur_end))
    73	                cur_start, cur_end = s, e
    74	        merged.append((chrom, cur_start, cur_end))
    75	    return merged
    76	
    77	
    78	def write_regions_fasta(regions, genome_seqs, out_fa):
    79	    records = []
    80	    id_map = {}
    81	    for chrom, start, end in regions:
    82	        seq = genome_seqs[chrom][start:end]
    83	        if not seq:
    84	            continue
    85	        seq_id = f"{chrom}__{start}__{end}"
    86	        records.append((seq_id, seq))
    87	        id_map[seq_id] = (chrom, start, end)
    88	    if records:
    89	        write_fasta(records, out_fa)
    90	    else:
    91	        open(out_fa, "w").close()
    92	    return id_map
    93	
    94	
    95	def prefix_attrs(attr_str, seq_id):
    96	    if not attr_str:
    97	        return attr_str
    98	    out = []
    99	    for attr in attr_str.split(";"):
   100	        if "=" in attr:
   101	            k, v = attr.split("=", 1)
   102	            if k in ("ID", "Parent"):
   103	                v = ",".join(f"{seq_id}_{item}" for item in v.split(",") if item)
   104	            out.append(f"{k}={v}")
   105	        else:
   106	            out.append(attr)
   107	    return ";".join(out)
   108	
   109	
   110	def adjust_gff(in_gff, out_gff, id_map):
   111	    with open(out_gff, "w") as out:
   112	        out.write("##gff-version 3\n")
   113	        if not os.path.exists(in_gff) or os.path.getsize(in_gff) == 0:
   114	            return
   115	        with open(in_gff, "r") as f:
   116	            for line in f:
   117	                if not line.strip() or line.startswith("#"):
   118	                    continue
   119	                parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
   120	                if len(parts) < 9:
   121	                    continue
   122	                seq_id = parts[0]
   123	                if seq_id not in id_map:
   124	                    continue
   125	                chrom, offset, _ = id_map[seq_id]
   126	                try:
   127	                    parts[3] = str(int(parts[3]) + offset)
   128	                    parts[4] = str(int(parts[4]) + offset)
   129	                except ValueError:
   130	                    pass
   131	                parts[0] = chrom
   132	                parts[8] = prefix_attrs(parts[8], seq_id)
   133	                out.write("\t".join(parts) + "\n")
   134	
   135	
   136	def adjust_proteins(in_faa, out_faa, id_map):
   137	    if not os.path.exists(in_faa) or os.path.getsize(in_faa) == 0:
   138	        open(out_faa, "w").close()
   139	        return
   140	    records = []
   141	    for raw_header, _, seq in parse_fasta(in_faa):
   142	        parts = raw_header.split(" # ")
   143	        if len(parts) >= 4:
   144	            token = parts[0]
   145	            base_seqid = token.rsplit("_", 1)[0]
   146	            mapping = id_map.get(base_seqid)
   147	            if mapping:
   148	                _, offset, _ = mapping
   149	                try:
   150	                    parts[1] = str(int(parts[1]) + offset)
   151	                    parts[2] = str(int(parts[2]) + offset)
   152	                except ValueError:
   153	                    pass
   154	                if len(parts) >= 5:
   155	                    parts[4] = prefix_attrs(parts[4], base_seqid)
   156	                raw_header = " # ".join(parts)
   157	        records.append((raw_header, seq))
   158	    write_fasta(records, out_faa)
   159	
   160	
   161	def run_prodigal(fasta_in, faa_out, gff_out):
   162	    cmd = [
   163	        "prodigal",
   164	        "-i", fasta_in,
   165	        "-a", faa_out,
   166	        "-f", "gff",
   167	        "-o", gff_out,
   168	        "-p", "meta",
   169	        "-q"
   170	    ]
   171	    subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
   172	
   173	
   174	def main():
   175	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run Prodigal on GOI-flanking regions only")
   176	    parser.add_argument("--genome", required=True, help="Genome FASTA")
   177	    parser.add_argument("--goi_bed", required=True, help="GOI bed file (hits)")
   178	    parser.add_argument("--window", type=int, default=50000, help="Flanking window around hits")
   179	    parser.add_argument("--output_faa", required=True, help="Output proteins FASTA")
   180	    parser.add_argument("--output_gff", required=True, help="Output GFF")
   181	    parser.add_argument("--fallback_full_genome", type=str2bool, default=False,
   182	                        help="Fallback to full-genome Prodigal if no regions found")
   183	    args = parser.parse_args()
   184	
   185	    genome_seqs = load_genome(args.genome)
   186	    regions = parse_bed(args.goi_bed)
   187	
   188	    if regions:
   189	        regions = merge_regions(regions, genome_seqs, max(0, args.window))
   190	    elif args.fallback_full_genome:
   191	        regions = [(chrom, 0, len(seq)) for chrom, seq in genome_seqs.items()]
   192	    else:
   193	        # No GOI regions and fallback disabled: produce empty outputs
   194	        open(args.output_faa, "w").close()
   195	        open(args.output_gff, "w").close()
   196	        print("No GOI regions found; skipping Prodigal.", file=sys.stderr)
   197	        return
   198	
   199	    if not regions:
   200	        open(args.output_faa, "w").close()
   201	        open(args.output_gff, "w").close()
   202	        print("No valid regions after merging; skipping Prodigal.", file=sys.stderr)
   203	        return
   204	
   205	    tmp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="synterra_prodigal_")
   206	    try:
   207	        regions_fa = os.path.join(tmp_dir, "regions.fna")
   208	        tmp_faa = os.path.join(tmp_dir, "prodigal.faa")
   209	        tmp_gff = os.path.join(tmp_dir, "prodigal.gff")
   210	
   211	        id_map = write_regions_fasta(regions, genome_seqs, regions_fa)
   212	        if not id_map:
   213	            open(args.output_faa, "w").close()
   214	            open(args.output_gff, "w").close()
   215	            print("No regions produced for Prodigal.", file=sys.stderr)
   216	            return
   217	
   218	        run_prodigal(regions_fa, tmp_faa, tmp_gff)
   219	
   220	        adjust_proteins(tmp_faa, args.output_faa, id_map)
   221	        adjust_gff(tmp_gff, args.output_gff, id_map)
   222	    finally:
   223	        shutil.rmtree(tmp_dir, ignore_errors=True)
   224	
   225	
   226	if __name__ == "__main__":
   227	    main()


===== ./bin/resolve_gene_input.py =====
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""
     3	Resolve gene input: accepts UniProt ID, NCBI protein accession, or FASTA file.
     4	Outputs JSON with resolved FASTA path + species name.
     5	"""
     6	
     7	import argparse
     8	import json
     9	import os
    10	import re
    11	import shutil
    12	import subprocess
    13	import sys
    14	import urllib.request
    15	import urllib.error
    16	from pathlib import Path
    17	
    18	
    19	# ─── Input Type Detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    20	
    21	# UniProt accession patterns (https://www.uniprot.org/help/accession_numbers)
    22	UNIPROT_RE = re.compile(
    23	    r'^[OPQ][0-9][A-Z0-9]{3}[0-9]$|'
    24	    r'^[A-NR-Z][0-9]([A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}[0-9]){1,2}$'
    25	)
    26	
    27	# NCBI RefSeq protein accessions
    28	NCBI_REFSEQ_RE = re.compile(r'^[XNWY]P_\d+\.\d+$')
    29	
    30	# NCBI GenBank protein accessions  (e.g., KAF1234567.1, AAB12345.1)
    31	NCBI_GENBANK_RE = re.compile(r'^[A-Z]{3}\d{5,}\.\d+$')
    32	
    33	
    34	def detect_input_type(gene_input):
    35	    """Detect whether input is a file, UniProt ID, or NCBI accession."""
    36	    # Check if it's a file on disk
    37	    if os.path.isfile(gene_input):
    38	        return 'file', gene_input
    39	    
    40	    # Strip version suffix for matching (e.g., P01501.2 → P01501)
    41	    clean = gene_input.strip().split('.')[0] if '.' in gene_input else gene_input.strip()
    42	    
    43	    if UNIPROT_RE.match(clean):
    44	        return 'uniprot', gene_input.strip()
    45	    
    46	    if NCBI_REFSEQ_RE.match(gene_input.strip()):
    47	        return 'ncbi', gene_input.strip()
    48	    
    49	    if NCBI_GENBANK_RE.match(gene_input.strip()):
    50	        return 'ncbi', gene_input.strip()
    51	    
    52	    # If nothing matched, treat as file path (will error downstream if not found)
    53	    return 'file', gene_input
    54	
    55	
    56	# ─── UniProt Fetching ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    57	
    58	def fetch_uniprot(uniprot_id, output_dir):
    59	    """Fetch sequence and species from UniProt REST API."""
    60	    base_url = "https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb"
    61	    
    62	    # Fetch JSON metadata (species, protein name, etc.)
    63	    json_url = f"{base_url}/{uniprot_id}.json"
    64	    print(f"  Fetching metadata from UniProt: {json_url}")
    65	    
    66	    try:
    67	        req = urllib.request.Request(json_url, headers={'Accept': 'application/json'})
    68	        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
    69	            data = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
    70	    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    71	        if e.code == 404:
    72	            print(f"  ERROR: UniProt ID '{uniprot_id}' not found", file=sys.stderr)
    73	            return None
    74	        raise
    75	    
    76	    # Extract species
    77	    organism = data.get('organism', {})
    78	    species = organism.get('scientificName', '')
    79	    taxid = organism.get('taxonId', '')
    80	    
    81	    # Extract protein name
    82	    protein_desc = data.get('proteinDescription', {})
    83	    rec_name = protein_desc.get('recommendedName', {})
    84	    protein_name = rec_name.get('fullName', {}).get('value', '')
    85	    if not protein_name:
    86	        # Try submittedName
    87	        sub_names = protein_desc.get('submissionNames', [])
    88	        if sub_names:
    89	            protein_name = sub_names[0].get('fullName', {}).get('value', '')
    90	    
    91	    # Fetch FASTA
    92	    fasta_url = f"{base_url}/{uniprot_id}.fasta"
    93	    print(f"  Fetching FASTA from UniProt: {fasta_url}")
    94	    
    95	    try:
    96	        with urllib.request.urlopen(fasta_url, timeout=30) as resp:
    97	            fasta_content = resp.read().decode()
    98	    except urllib.error.HTTPError:
    99	        print(f"  ERROR: Could not fetch FASTA for '{uniprot_id}'", file=sys.stderr)
   100	        return None
   101	    
   102	    if not fasta_content.strip():
   103	        print(f"  ERROR: Empty FASTA for '{uniprot_id}'", file=sys.stderr)
   104	        return None
   105	    
   106	    # Write FASTA
   107	    fasta_path = Path(output_dir) / f"{uniprot_id}.fasta"
   108	    fasta_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   109	    fasta_path.write_text(fasta_content)
   110	    
   111	    print(f"  Species: {species}")
   112	    print(f"  Protein: {protein_name}")
   113	    print(f"  TaxID:   {taxid}")
   114	    print(f"  FASTA:   {fasta_path}")
   115	    
   116	    return {
   117	        'fasta_path': str(fasta_path),
   118	        'species': species,
   119	        'taxid': str(taxid),
   120	        'protein_name': protein_name,
   121	        'source': 'uniprot',
   122	        'input_id': uniprot_id
   123	    }
   124	
   125	
   126	# ─── NCBI Fetching ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   127	
   128	def fetch_ncbi(accession, output_dir):
   129	    """Fetch sequence and species from NCBI."""
   130	    print(f"  Fetching from NCBI: {accession}")
   131	    
   132	    # Fetch FASTA
   133	    try:
   134	        result = subprocess.run(
   135	            ['efetch', '-db', 'protein', '-id', accession, '-format', 'fasta'],
   136	            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True, timeout=30
   137	        )
   138	        fasta_content = result.stdout.strip()
   139	    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError) as e:
   140	        print(f"  ERROR: Could not fetch FASTA from NCBI: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   141	        return None
   142	    
   143	    if not fasta_content:
   144	        print(f"  ERROR: Empty FASTA for '{accession}'", file=sys.stderr)
   145	        return None
   146	    
   147	    # Fetch metadata (organism name)
   148	    species = ''
   149	    protein_name = ''
   150	    taxid = ''
   151	    try:
   152	        result = subprocess.run(
   153	            ['efetch', '-db', 'protein', '-id', accession, '-format', 'gpc'],
   154	            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True, timeout=30
   155	        )
   156	        gpc_output = result.stdout
   157	        
   158	        # Parse organism from GPC XML
   159	        import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
   160	        try:
   161	            root = ET.fromstring(gpc_output)
   162	            # Look for INSDSeq_organism
   163	            for elem in root.iter():
   164	                if elem.tag == 'INSDSeq_organism' and elem.text:
   165	                    species = elem.text.strip()
   166	                elif elem.tag == 'INSDSeq_definition' and elem.text:
   167	                    protein_name = elem.text.strip()
   168	                elif elem.tag == 'INSDSeq_taxonomy' and elem.text:
   169	                    pass  # Full taxonomy string, not needed
   170	        except ET.ParseError:
   171	            pass
   172	        
   173	        # Get TaxID via esearch if we have species
   174	        if species:
   175	            try:
   176	                tax_result = subprocess.run(
   177	                    ['esearch', '-db', 'taxonomy', '-query', species],
   178	                    capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15,
   179	                    stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL
   180	                )
   181	                efetch_result = subprocess.run(
   182	                    ['efetch', '-format', 'uid'],
   183	                    input=tax_result.stdout, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15
   184	                )
   185	                taxid = efetch_result.stdout.strip()
   186	            except Exception:
   187	                pass
   188	                
   189	    except Exception as e:
   190	        print(f"  Warning: Could not fetch metadata: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   191	    
   192	    # Write FASTA
   193	    safe_name = accession.replace('.', '_')
   194	    fasta_path = Path(output_dir) / f"{safe_name}.fasta"
   195	    fasta_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   196	    fasta_path.write_text(fasta_content + '\n')
   197	    
   198	    print(f"  Species: {species}")
   199	    print(f"  Protein: {protein_name}")
   200	    print(f"  TaxID:   {taxid}")
   201	    print(f"  FASTA:   {fasta_path}")
   202	    
   203	    return {
   204	        'fasta_path': str(fasta_path),
   205	        'species': species,
   206	        'taxid': taxid,
   207	        'protein_name': protein_name,
   208	        'source': 'ncbi',
   209	        'input_id': accession
   210	    }
   211	
   212	
   213	# ─── FASTA File Handling ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   214	
   215	def handle_fasta_file(fasta_path, output_dir, species=None):
   216	    """Handle a local FASTA file."""
   217	    if not os.path.isfile(fasta_path):
   218	        print(f"ERROR: File not found: {fasta_path}", file=sys.stderr)
   219	        return None
   220	
   221	    # Keep all resolved inputs in one place with a predictable extension so
   222	    # Nextflow output collection (`resolved_query/*.fasta`) always works.
   223	    outdir = Path(output_dir)
   224	    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   225	    copied_fasta = outdir / "input_query.fasta"
   226	    shutil.copyfile(fasta_path, copied_fasta)
   227	    
   228	    return {
   229	        'fasta_path': str(copied_fasta.resolve()),
   230	        'species': species or '',
   231	        'taxid': '',
   232	        'protein_name': '',
   233	        'source': 'file',
   234	        'input_id': Path(fasta_path).name
   235	    }
   236	
   237	
   238	# ─── Main ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   239	
   240	def main():
   241	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   242	        description="Resolve gene input: UniProt ID, NCBI accession, or FASTA file",
   243	        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
   244	        epilog="""
   245	Examples:
   246	  %(prog)s --input P01501                        # UniProt ID
   247	  %(prog)s --input XP_006565763.1                # NCBI RefSeq protein
   248	  %(prog)s --input my_protein.fasta --species "Apis mellifera"  # FASTA file
   249	"""
   250	    )
   251	    
   252	    parser.add_argument("--input", required=True,
   253	                       help="Gene input: UniProt ID, NCBI protein accession, or path to FASTA file")
   254	    parser.add_argument("--species", default=None,
   255	                       help="Override species name (required for FASTA files without --query_id)")
   256	    parser.add_argument("--outdir", default="resolved_query",
   257	                       help="Output directory for resolved FASTA (default: resolved_query)")
   258	    
   259	    args = parser.parse_args()
   260	    
   261	    # Detect input type
   262	    input_type, clean_input = detect_input_type(args.input)
   263	    print(f"Input: '{args.input}' → detected as: {input_type}")
   264	    
   265	    # Resolve
   266	    result = None
   267	    
   268	    if input_type == 'uniprot':
   269	        result = fetch_uniprot(clean_input, args.outdir)
   270	        
   271	    elif input_type == 'ncbi':
   272	        result = fetch_ncbi(clean_input, args.outdir)
   273	        
   274	    elif input_type == 'file':
   275	        result = handle_fasta_file(clean_input, output_dir=args.outdir, species=args.species)
   276	    
   277	    if result is None:
   278	        print("ERROR: Failed to resolve gene input", file=sys.stderr)
   279	        sys.exit(1)
   280	    
   281	    # Override species if provided on command line
   282	    if args.species:
   283	        result['species'] = args.species
   284	    
   285	    # Validate: if no species, warn (but don't fail — main.nf handles this)
   286	    if not result['species']:
   287	        print("WARNING: No species detected. --home_species will be required.", file=sys.stderr)
   288	    
   289	    # Write JSON result
   290	    output_path = Path(args.outdir)
   291	    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   292	    
   293	    json_path = output_path / "resolved_input.json"
   294	    with open(json_path, 'w') as f:
   295	        json.dump(result, f, indent=2)
   296	    
   297	    # Also write plain-text outputs for easy Nextflow consumption
   298	    (output_path / "resolved_species.txt").write_text(result['species'])
   299	    (output_path / "resolved_fasta.txt").write_text(result['fasta_path'])
   300	    
   301	    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
   302	    print(f"Resolved: {result['source']} → {result['species'] or '(no species)'}")
   303	    print(f"  FASTA: {result['fasta_path']}")
   304	    print(f"  JSON:  {json_path}")
   305	    print(f"{'='*60}")
   306	    
   307	    # Print JSON to stdout for piping
   308	    print(json.dumps(result))
   309	
   310	
   311	if __name__ == "__main__":
   312	    main()


===== ./bin/sequence_utils.py =====
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""
     3	sequence_utils.py - Robust sequence handling without external dependencies
     4	
     5	Philosophy: Assume nothing. Work with what's there. If nothing is there, number sequentially.
     6	
     7	Handles:
     8	- FASTA parsing (no BioPython)
     9	- GFF parsing (no dependencies)
    10	- ID extraction from any format (NCBI, Ensembl, custom, unknown)
    11	- Fallback to sequential numbering
    12	"""
    13	
    14	import re
    15	import hashlib
    16	from pathlib import Path
    17	from typing import Iterator, Dict, List, Tuple, Optional, Any
    18	
    19	
    20	# =============================================================================
    21	# FASTA PARSING (No BioPython)
    22	# =============================================================================
    23	
    24	def parse_fasta(filepath: str) -> Iterator[Tuple[str, str, str]]:
    25	    """
    26	    Parse FASTA file yielding (raw_header, clean_id, sequence) tuples.
    27	    
    28	    Works with any FASTA format. No assumptions about header structure.
    29	    """
    30	    current_header = None
    31	    current_seq = []
    32	    
    33	    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
    34	        for line in f:
    35	            line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
    36	            if line.startswith('>'):
    37	                if current_header is not None:
    38	                    seq = ''.join(current_seq)
    39	                    yield current_header, extract_id(current_header), seq
    40	                current_header = line[1:]  # Remove '>'
    41	                current_seq = []
    42	            elif line and current_header is not None:
    43	                # Skip empty lines, handle sequence
    44	                current_seq.append(line.strip())
    45	        
    46	        # Don't forget the last sequence
    47	        if current_header is not None:
    48	            seq = ''.join(current_seq)
    49	            yield current_header, extract_id(current_header), seq
    50	
    51	
    52	def parse_fasta_to_dict(filepath: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
    53	    """Parse FASTA to dict {id: sequence}."""
    54	    return {clean_id: seq for _, clean_id, seq in parse_fasta(filepath)}
    55	
    56	
    57	def write_fasta(records: List[Tuple[str, str]], filepath: str, wrap: int = 80):
    58	    """
    59	    Write FASTA file.
    60	    
    61	    Args:
    62	        records: List of (header, sequence) tuples
    63	        filepath: Output path
    64	        wrap: Line wrap width (0 = no wrap)
    65	    """
    66	    with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
    67	        for header, seq in records:
    68	            # Ensure header doesn't start with '>'
    69	            header = header.lstrip('>')
    70	            f.write(f'>{header}\n')
    71	            
    72	            if wrap > 0:
    73	                for i in range(0, len(seq), wrap):
    74	                    f.write(seq[i:i+wrap] + '\n')
    75	            else:
    76	                f.write(seq + '\n')
    77	
    78	
    79	def count_sequences(filepath: str) -> int:
    80	    """Count sequences in FASTA file efficiently."""
    81	    count = 0
    82	    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
    83	        for line in f:
    84	            if line.startswith('>'):
    85	                count += 1
    86	    return count
    87	
    88	
    89	# =============================================================================
    90	# ID EXTRACTION - The heart of robust parsing
    91	# =============================================================================
    92	
    93	# Known ID patterns (order matters - more specific first)
    94	ID_PATTERNS = [
    95	    # NCBI RefSeq proteins: XP_123456.1, NP_123456.1, YP_123456.1, WP_123456.1
    96	    (r'^([XNYWZ]P_\d+(?:\.\d+)?)', 'ncbi_refseq'),
    97	    
    98	    # NCBI GenBank proteins: AAA12345.1
    99	    # Require token boundary after accession so long IDs like LOC143834063 are
   100	    # not incorrectly truncated to LOC14383.
   101	    (r'^([A-Z]{3}\d{5}(?:\.\d+)?)(?=$|[\s|])', 'ncbi_genbank'),
   102	    
   103	    # NCBI GI (legacy): gi|12345|
   104	    (r'^gi\|(\d+)\|', 'ncbi_gi'),
   105	    
   106	    # NCBI accession with version: NC_123456.1, NZ_CP123456.1
   107	    (r'^((?:NC|NZ|NW|NT|AC|NG)_[A-Z]*\d+(?:\.\d+)?)', 'ncbi_nucl'),
   108	    
   109	    # NCBI assembly accession in sequence name: lcl|NC_123456.1_cds_XP_123456.1_1
   110	    (r'cds_([XNYWZ]P_\d+(?:\.\d+)?)_', 'ncbi_cds'),
   111	    
   112	    # UniProt: P12345, Q9NXB0, A0A1B2C3D4
   113	    (r'^([OPQ][0-9][A-Z0-9]{3}[0-9]|[A-NR-Z][0-9]([A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}[0-9]){1,2})', 'uniprot'),
   114	    
   115	    # UniProt with prefix: sp|P12345|NAME, tr|A0A123|NAME
   116	    (r'^(?:sp|tr)\|([^\|]+)\|', 'uniprot_full'),
   117	    
   118	    # Ensembl proteins: ENSP00000123456, ENSMUSP00000123456
   119	    (r'^(ENS[A-Z]*P\d+(?:\.\d+)?)', 'ensembl_protein'),
   120	    
   121	    # Ensembl genes: ENSG00000123456
   122	    (r'^(ENS[A-Z]*G\d+(?:\.\d+)?)', 'ensembl_gene'),
   123	    
   124	    # Ensembl transcripts: ENST00000123456
   125	    (r'^(ENS[A-Z]*T\d+(?:\.\d+)?)', 'ensembl_transcript'),
   126	    
   127	    # FlyBase: FBgn0000001, FBpp0000001
   128	    (r'^(FB[a-z]{2}\d+)', 'flybase'),
   129	    
   130	    # WormBase: WBGene00000001
   131	    (r'^(WBGene\d+)', 'wormbase'),
   132	    
   133	    # TAIR (Arabidopsis): AT1G01010.1
   134	    (r'^(AT[1-5MC]G\d+(?:\.\d+)?)', 'tair'),
   135	    
   136	    # Prodigal format: >1_1 # start # end # strand # ID=1_1;...
   137	    (r'^(\d+_\d+)\s*#', 'prodigal'),
   138	    
   139	    # Generic locus_tag pattern: XXXX_12345
   140	    (r'^([A-Z]{2,6}_\d+)', 'locus_tag'),
   141	    
   142	    # Generic gene ID with underscore: gene_12345
   143	    (r'^(gene_\d+)', 'generic_gene'),
   144	    
   145	    # Generic protein ID: protein_12345, prot_12345
   146	    (r'^(prot(?:ein)?_\d+)', 'generic_protein'),
   147	    
   148	    # Just take first word if nothing else matches
   149	    (r'^(\S+)', 'first_word'),
   150	]
   151	
   152	
   153	def extract_id(header: str, fallback_counter: Optional[int] = None) -> str:
   154	    """
   155	    Extract a clean, usable ID from any FASTA header.
   156	    
   157	    Philosophy:
   158	    1. Try known patterns (NCBI, Ensembl, UniProt, etc.)
   159	    2. Fall back to first non-whitespace token
   160	    3. If still nothing, use counter-based ID
   161	    
   162	    Args:
   163	        header: FASTA header (without '>')
   164	        fallback_counter: Optional counter for generating fallback IDs
   165	        
   166	    Returns:
   167	        Clean ID string
   168	    """
   169	    if not header or not header.strip():
   170	        if fallback_counter is not None:
   171	            return f"seq_{fallback_counter}"
   172	        return "unknown"
   173	    
   174	    header = header.strip()
   175	    
   176	    # Try each pattern
   177	    for pattern, id_type in ID_PATTERNS:
   178	        match = re.search(pattern, header)
   179	        if match:
   180	            extracted = match.group(1)
   181	            # Clean up common issues
   182	            extracted = extracted.strip('|').strip()
   183	            if extracted:
   184	                return extracted
   185	    
   186	    # Last resort: hash the header
   187	    if fallback_counter is not None:
   188	        return f"seq_{fallback_counter}"
   189	    
   190	    # Generate deterministic ID from header
   191	    hash_id = hashlib.md5(header.encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
   192	    return f"seq_{hash_id}"
   193	
   194	
   195	def extract_base_id(id_string: str) -> str:
   196	    """
   197	    Extract the base gene/protein ID, stripping variants, fragments, versions.
   198	    
   199	    Examples:
   200	        "XP_123456.1" -> "XP_123456"
   201	        "gene|var_1" -> "gene"
   202	        "gene|frag_half_1" -> "gene"
   203	        "ENSP00000123456.2" -> "ENSP00000123456"
   204	        "lcl|NC_123_cds_XP_456.1_7" -> "XP_456"
   205	    """
   206	    if not id_string:
   207	        return id_string
   208	    
   209	    original = id_string
   210	    
   211	    # Remove our own variant/fragment suffixes
   212	    if '|' in id_string:
   213	        parts = id_string.split('|')
   214	        # Check if last part is a variant/fragment marker
   215	        if len(parts) >= 2:
   216	            last = parts[-1]
   217	            if any(last.startswith(p) for p in ['var_', 'frag_', 'half_', 'third_', 'quarter_']):
   218	                id_string = '|'.join(parts[:-1])
   219	            elif re.match(r'^(var|frag|half|third|quarter)', last):
   220	                id_string = '|'.join(parts[:-1])
   221	    
   222	    # Handle lcl|..._cds_XP_123.1_N format
   223	    cds_match = re.search(r'cds_([XNYWZ]P_\d+)', id_string)
   224	    if cds_match:
   225	        id_string = cds_match.group(1)
   226	    
   227	    # Remove version numbers (.1, .2, etc.) from the end
   228	    id_string = re.sub(r'\.\d+$', '', id_string)
   229	    
   230	    # Remove trailing underscore + number (like _1, _2 for CDS copies)
   231	    # But protect NCBI accession prefixes (XP_, NP_, WP_, YP_, XM_, NM_, NC_, etc.)
   232	    if not re.match(r'^[A-Z]{1,2}P_\d+$', id_string) and not re.match(r'^[A-Z]{2}_\d+$', id_string):
   233	        id_string = re.sub(r'_\d+$', '', id_string)
   234	    
   235	    return id_string if id_string else original
   236	
   237	
   238	def normalize_id(id_string: str) -> str:
   239	    """
   240	    Normalize ID for comparison (lowercase, no special chars).
   241	    """
   242	    if not id_string:
   243	        return ""
   244	    return re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]', '', id_string.lower())
   245	
   246	
   247	def ids_match(id1: str, id2: str) -> bool:
   248	    """
   249	    Check if two IDs refer to the same entity (fuzzy matching).
   250	    """
   251	    if not id1 or not id2:
   252	        return False
   253	    
   254	    # Exact match
   255	    if id1 == id2:
   256	        return True
   257	    
   258	    # Base ID match
   259	    base1 = extract_base_id(id1)
   260	    base2 = extract_base_id(id2)
   261	    if base1 == base2:
   262	        return True
   263	    
   264	    # Normalized match
   265	    if normalize_id(base1) == normalize_id(base2):
   266	        return True
   267	    
   268	    # One contains the other (for complex IDs) - require minimum length to avoid false positives
   269	    if len(base1) >= 6 and len(base2) >= 6:
   270	        if base1 in id2 or base2 in id1:
   271	            return True
   272	    
   273	    return False
   274	
   275	
   276	# =============================================================================
   277	# GFF PARSING (No dependencies)
   278	# =============================================================================
   279	
   280	def parse_gff(filepath: str, feature_types: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> Iterator[Dict[str, Any]]:
   281	    """
   282	    Parse GFF/GFF3/GTF file yielding feature dictionaries.
   283	    
   284	    Works with any GFF-like format. Handles:
   285	    - GFF3 (ID=...; Parent=...)
   286	    - GTF (gene_id "..."; transcript_id "...")
   287	    - Edge cases and malformed files
   288	    
   289	    Args:
   290	        filepath: Path to GFF file
   291	        feature_types: Optional list of feature types to include (e.g., ['CDS', 'gene'])
   292	        
   293	    Yields:
   294	        Dict with keys: seqid, source, type, start, end, score, strand, phase, attributes
   295	    """
   296	    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   297	        for line_num, line in enumerate(f, 1):
   298	            line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
   299	            
   300	            # Skip comments and empty lines
   301	            if not line or line.startswith('#'):
   302	                continue
   303	            
   304	            parts = line.split('\t')
   305	            if len(parts) < 8:
   306	                continue  # Malformed line, skip silently
   307	            
   308	            feature_type = parts[2]
   309	            
   310	            # Filter by feature type if specified
   311	            if feature_types and feature_type not in feature_types:
   312	                continue
   313	            
   314	            # Parse coordinates
   315	            try:
   316	                start = int(parts[3])
   317	                end = int(parts[4])
   318	            except ValueError:
   319	                continue  # Invalid coordinates
   320	            
   321	            # Parse score
   322	            score = None
   323	            if parts[5] != '.':
   324	                try:
   325	                    score = float(parts[5])
   326	                except ValueError:
   327	                    pass
   328	            
   329	            # Parse phase
   330	            phase = None
   331	            if len(parts) > 7 and parts[7] != '.':
   332	                try:
   333	                    phase = int(parts[7])
   334	                except ValueError:
   335	                    pass
   336	            
   337	            # Parse attributes
   338	            attributes = {}
   339	            if len(parts) > 8:
   340	                attributes = parse_gff_attributes(parts[8])
   341	            
   342	            yield {
   343	                'seqid': parts[0],
   344	                'source': parts[1],
   345	                'type': feature_type,
   346	                'start': start,
   347	                'end': end,
   348	                'score': score,
   349	                'strand': parts[6] if parts[6] in ['+', '-'] else '.',
   350	                'phase': phase,
   351	                'attributes': attributes,
   352	                'line_num': line_num
   353	            }
   354	
   355	
   356	def parse_gff_attributes(attr_string: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
   357	    """
   358	    Parse GFF3/GTF attribute string into dictionary.
   359	    
   360	    Handles both:
   361	    - GFF3: ID=gene1;Name=ABC;Parent=transcript1
   362	    - GTF: gene_id "gene1"; transcript_id "tx1";
   363	    """
   364	    attributes = {}
   365	    
   366	    if not attr_string or attr_string == '.':
   367	        return attributes
   368	    
   369	    # Try GFF3 format first (key=value;)
   370	    if '=' in attr_string:
   371	        for pair in attr_string.split(';'):
   372	            pair = pair.strip()
   373	            if '=' in pair:
   374	                key, value = pair.split('=', 1)
   375	                # URL decode common escapes
   376	                value = value.replace('%3B', ';').replace('%3D', '=')
   377	                value = value.replace('%26', '&').replace('%2C', ',')
   378	                attributes[key.strip()] = value.strip()
   379	    
   380	    # Try GTF format (key "value";)
   381	    elif '"' in attr_string:
   382	        for match in re.finditer(r'(\S+)\s+"([^"]*)"', attr_string):
   383	            key, value = match.groups()
   384	            attributes[key] = value
   385	    
   386	    return attributes
   387	
   388	
   389	def get_feature_id(feature: Dict[str, Any], fallback_counter: Optional[int] = None) -> str:
   390	    """
   391	    Get a usable ID from a GFF feature.
   392	    
   393	    Tries in order:
   394	    1. ID attribute
   395	    2. gene_id, transcript_id, protein_id
   396	    3. Name attribute
   397	    4. locus_tag
   398	    5. Generate from position
   399	    """
   400	    attrs = feature.get('attributes', {})
   401	    
   402	    # Try standard ID attributes
   403	    for key in ['ID', 'gene_id', 'transcript_id', 'protein_id', 'Name', 'locus_tag', 'gene']:
   404	        if key in attrs and attrs[key]:
   405	            return attrs[key]
   406	    
   407	    # Generate from position
   408	    if fallback_counter is not None:
   409	        return f"{feature['type']}_{fallback_counter}"
   410	    
   411	    seqid = feature.get('seqid', 'unknown')
   412	    start = feature.get('start', 0)
   413	    return f"{feature['type']}_{seqid}_{start}"
   414	
   415	
   416	# =============================================================================
   417	# SEQUENCE OPERATIONS
   418	# =============================================================================
   419	
   420	def reverse_complement(seq: str) -> str:
   421	    """Reverse complement a DNA sequence."""
   422	    complement = {'A': 'T', 'T': 'A', 'G': 'C', 'C': 'G',
   423	                  'a': 't', 't': 'a', 'g': 'c', 'c': 'g',
   424	                  'N': 'N', 'n': 'n',
   425	                  'R': 'Y', 'Y': 'R', 'S': 'S', 'W': 'W',
   426	                  'K': 'M', 'M': 'K', 'B': 'V', 'V': 'B',
   427	                  'D': 'H', 'H': 'D'}
   428	    return ''.join(complement.get(base, 'N') for base in reversed(seq))
   429	
   430	
   431	def translate(seq: str, table: int = 1) -> str:
   432	    """
   433	    Translate DNA to protein.
   434	    
   435	    Args:
   436	        seq: DNA sequence
   437	        table: Genetic code table (1 = standard, 11 = bacterial)
   438	    """
   439	    # Standard genetic code
   440	    codon_table = {
   441	        'TTT': 'F', 'TTC': 'F', 'TTA': 'L', 'TTG': 'L',
   442	        'TCT': 'S', 'TCC': 'S', 'TCA': 'S', 'TCG': 'S',
   443	        'TAT': 'Y', 'TAC': 'Y', 'TAA': '*', 'TAG': '*',
   444	        'TGT': 'C', 'TGC': 'C', 'TGA': '*', 'TGG': 'W',
   445	        'CTT': 'L', 'CTC': 'L', 'CTA': 'L', 'CTG': 'L',
   446	        'CCT': 'P', 'CCC': 'P', 'CCA': 'P', 'CCG': 'P',
   447	        'CAT': 'H', 'CAC': 'H', 'CAA': 'Q', 'CAG': 'Q',
   448	        'CGT': 'R', 'CGC': 'R', 'CGA': 'R', 'CGG': 'R',
   449	        'ATT': 'I', 'ATC': 'I', 'ATA': 'I', 'ATG': 'M',
   450	        'ACT': 'T', 'ACC': 'T', 'ACA': 'T', 'ACG': 'T',
   451	        'AAT': 'N', 'AAC': 'N', 'AAA': 'K', 'AAG': 'K',
   452	        'AGT': 'S', 'AGC': 'S', 'AGA': 'R', 'AGG': 'R',
   453	        'GTT': 'V', 'GTC': 'V', 'GTA': 'V', 'GTG': 'V',
   454	        'GCT': 'A', 'GCC': 'A', 'GCA': 'A', 'GCG': 'A',
   455	        'GAT': 'D', 'GAC': 'D', 'GAA': 'E', 'GAG': 'E',
   456	        'GGT': 'G', 'GGC': 'G', 'GGA': 'G', 'GGG': 'G',
   457	    }
   458	    
   459	    seq = seq.upper().replace('U', 'T')
   460	    protein = []
   461	    
   462	    for i in range(0, len(seq) - 2, 3):
   463	        codon = seq[i:i+3]
   464	        if 'N' in codon:
   465	            protein.append('X')
   466	        else:
   467	            protein.append(codon_table.get(codon, 'X'))
   468	    
   469	    return ''.join(protein)
   470	
   471	
   472	def extract_sequence(genome_dict: Dict[str, str], chrom: str, start: int, end: int, strand: str = '+') -> str:
   473	    """
   474	    Extract sequence from genome dictionary.
   475	    
   476	    Args:
   477	        genome_dict: Dict of {chrom: sequence}
   478	        chrom: Chromosome/contig name
   479	        start: 1-based start position
   480	        end: 1-based end position (inclusive)
   481	        strand: '+' or '-'
   482	    """
   483	    if chrom not in genome_dict:
   484	        return ""
   485	    
   486	    seq = genome_dict[chrom]
   487	    # Convert to 0-based for Python slicing
   488	    extracted = seq[start-1:end]
   489	    
   490	    if strand == '-':
   491	        extracted = reverse_complement(extracted)
   492	    
   493	    return extracted
   494	
   495	
   496	# =============================================================================
   497	# BATCH ID ASSIGNMENT
   498	# =============================================================================
   499	
   500	class IdAssigner:
   501	    """
   502	    Assigns clean IDs to sequences, ensuring uniqueness.
   503	    
   504	    Use when you have many sequences and need consistent, unique IDs.
   505	    """
   506	    
   507	    def __init__(self, prefix: str = "seq"):
   508	        self.prefix = prefix
   509	        self.seen_ids = {}
   510	        self.counter = 0
   511	    
   512	    def get_id(self, original_header: str) -> str:
   513	        """
   514	        Get a unique ID for a sequence.
   515	        
   516	        If the extracted ID was seen before, appends a number.
   517	        """
   518	        # Try to extract ID
   519	        base_id = extract_id(original_header)
   520	        
   521	        if base_id in self.seen_ids:
   522	            # Already seen, add suffix
   523	            self.seen_ids[base_id] += 1
   524	            return f"{base_id}_{self.seen_ids[base_id]}"
   525	        else:
   526	            self.seen_ids[base_id] = 0
   527	            return base_id
   528	    
   529	    def get_sequential_id(self) -> str:
   530	        """Get next sequential ID."""
   531	        self.counter += 1
   532	        return f"{self.prefix}_{self.counter}"
   533	
   534	
   535	# =============================================================================
   536	# CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS
   537	# =============================================================================
   538	
   539	def load_genome(filepath: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
   540	    """Load genome FASTA as dict {chrom: sequence}."""
   541	    return {clean_id: seq for _, clean_id, seq in parse_fasta(filepath)}
   542	
   543	
   544	def get_sequence_lengths(filepath: str) -> Dict[str, int]:
   545	    """Get lengths of all sequences in FASTA."""
   546	    return {clean_id: len(seq) for _, clean_id, seq in parse_fasta(filepath)}
   547	
   548	
   549	def filter_fasta(input_path: str, output_path: str, keep_ids: set):
   550	    """Filter FASTA to keep only specified IDs."""
   551	    records = []
   552	    for header, clean_id, seq in parse_fasta(input_path):
   553	        if clean_id in keep_ids or extract_base_id(clean_id) in keep_ids:
   554	            records.append((header, seq))
   555	    write_fasta(records, output_path)
   556	
   557	
   558	def merge_fastas(input_paths: List[str], output_path: str, deduplicate: bool = True):
   559	    """Merge multiple FASTA files."""
   560	    seen = set()
   561	    records = []
   562	    
   563	    for path in input_paths:
   564	        for header, clean_id, seq in parse_fasta(path):
   565	            if deduplicate:
   566	                if clean_id in seen:
   567	                    continue
   568	                seen.add(clean_id)
   569	            records.append((header, seq))
   570	    
   571	    write_fasta(records, output_path)
   572	
   573

   579	    import sys

   581	    # Test ID extraction
   582	    test_headers = [
   583	        "XP_123456.1 hypothetical protein",
   584	        "sp|P12345|ABC_HUMAN Some protein",
   585	        "ENSP00000123456.2 description",
   586	        "lcl|NC_001234.5_cds_XP_789012.1_15 gene description",
   587	        "gene|var_1",

   589	        "random_header_with no known pattern",
   590	        "ABC_12345 locus tag style",
   591	        "",
