===== ./install.sh =====
     1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# Installation script for SynTerra
     3	# Run this to set up your environment
     4	
     5	set -e
     6	
     7	echo "==========================================="
     8	echo "  SynTerra Installation Script"
     9	echo "==========================================="
    10	
    11	# Check for conda
    12	if ! command -v conda &> /dev/null; then
    13	    echo "❌ ERROR: Conda not found!"
    14	    echo "Please install Miniconda or Anaconda first:"
    15	    echo "  https://docs.conda.io/en/latest/miniconda.html"
    16	    exit 1
    17	fi
    18	
    19	echo "✓ Found conda"
    20	
    21	# Check for nextflow
    22	if ! command -v nextflow &> /dev/null; then
    23	    echo "⚠️  Nextflow not found. Installing..."
    24	    conda install -c bioconda nextflow -y
    25	else
    26	    echo "✓ Found nextflow"
    27	fi
    28	
    29	# Create conda environment
    30	echo ""
    31	echo "Creating conda environment 'syntenyfinder'..."
    32	if conda env list | grep -q syntenyfinder; then
    33	    echo "⚠️  Environment 'syntenyfinder' already exists"
    34	    read -p "Remove and recreate? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
    35	    echo
    36	    if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    37	        conda env remove -n syntenyfinder -y
    38	        conda env create -f environment.yml
    39	    fi
    40	else
    41	    conda env create -f environment.yml
    42	fi
    43	
    44	echo ""
    45	echo "✓ Environment created successfully"
    46	
    47	# Test installation
    48	echo ""
    49	echo "Testing installation..."
    50	source $(conda info --base)/etc/profile.d/conda.sh
    51	conda activate syntenyfinder
    52	
    53	# Check key tools
    54	echo "Checking dependencies..."
    55	for tool in mmseqs blast prodigal augustus plotly; do
    56	    if command -v $tool &> /dev/null || python -c "import $tool" 2>/dev/null; then
    57	        echo "  ✓ $tool"
    58	    else
    59	        echo "  ⚠️  $tool not found (may not be critical)"
    60	    fi
    61	done
    62	
    63	echo ""
    64	echo "==========================================="
    65	echo "  Installation Complete!"
    66	echo "==========================================="
    67	echo ""
    68	echo "To use SynTerra:"
    69	echo "  1. Activate environment:"
    70	echo "     conda activate syntenyfinder"
    71	echo ""
    72	echo "  2. Test with example data:"
    73	echo "     nextflow run main.nf -profile test"
    74	echo ""
    75	echo "  3. Run with your data:"
    76	echo "     nextflow run main.nf --gene gene.fasta --home_genome genome.fna --target_genomes 'targets/*.fna'"
    77	echo ""
    78	echo "For help:"
    79	echo "  - See README.md for overview"
    80	echo "  - See USAGE.md for detailed usage"
    81	echo "  - Run: nextflow run main.nf --help"
    82	echo ""


===== ./integration_test.sh =====
     1	#!/bin/bash
     2	set -e
     3	
     4	# SynTerra Integration Test
     5	# Run with: ./integration_test.sh [QUERY_FILE] [TARGET_DIR] [HOME_GENOME]
     6	
     7	QUERY=${1:-"test_data/query.fasta"}
     8	TARGET_DIR=${2:-"test_data/targets/"}
     9	HOME_GENOME=${3:-"test_data/home.fasta"}
    10	OUTDIR="results_test"
    11	
    12	echo "=== SynTerra Integration Test ==="
    13	echo "Query: $QUERY"
    14	echo "Targets: $TARGET_DIR"
    15	echo "Output: $OUTDIR"
    16	
    17	# Cleanup previous run
    18	rm -rf $OUTDIR .nextflow* work
    19	
    20	# Run Pipeline
    21	# Use 'stub' or 'test' profile if available, else run normal
    22	# Assuming we run normal execution on small test data
    23	echo "Running Nextflow..."
    24	nextflow run main.nf \
    25	    --gene "$QUERY" \
    26	    --target_genomes "$TARGET_DIR/*.fna" \
    27	    --home_genome "$HOME_GENOME" \
    28	    --mode pro \
    29	    --outdir "$OUTDIR" \
    30	    -resume
    31	
    32	echo "=== Verification ==="
    33	
    34	# 1. Check Region Files
    35	echo "Checking regions..."
    36	REGIONS_FOUND=$(find "$OUTDIR" -name "*.regions.bed" | wc -l)
    37	if [ "$REGIONS_FOUND" -gt 0 ]; then
    38	    echo "  [PASS] Found $REGIONS_FOUND region BED files."
    39	else
    40	    echo "  [FAIL] No region BED files found."
    41	    exit 1
    42	fi
    43	
    44	# 2. Check for Empty/Missing Augmented Results
    45	# Check if we have augmented outputs for at least one genome
    46	AUG_BEDS=$(find "$OUTDIR/augmented" -name "*.candidates.bed" | wc -l)
    47	if [ "$AUG_BEDS" -gt 0 ]; then
    48	    echo "  [PASS] Found $AUG_BEDS augmented search BED files."
    49	else
    50	    echo "  [FAIL] No augmented search BED files found."
    51	    exit 1
    52	fi
    53	
    54	# 3. Check Report
    55	REPORT="$OUTDIR/synterra_report.html"
    56	if [ -f "$REPORT" ]; then
    57	    echo "  [PASS] Report generated."
    58	    
    59	    # Check for gene count > 0 in report
    60	    # Simple grep for now, assuming JSON embedded or HTML table
    61	    # Just checking file size > 0
    62	    if [ -s "$REPORT" ]; then
    63	        echo "  [PASS] Report is not empty."
    64	    else
    65	        echo "  [FAIL] Report is empty."
    66	        exit 1
    67	    fi
    68	else
    69	    echo "  [FAIL] Report not found."
    70	    exit 1
    71	fi
    72	
    73	# 4. Check for Critical Errors in Log
    74	if [ -f ".nextflow.log" ]; then
    75	    ERRORS=$(grep -i "ERROR" .nextflow.log | grep -v "ErrorStrategy" | grep -v "ignore" | wc -l)
    76	    if [ "$ERRORS" -eq 0 ]; then
    77	        echo "  [PASS] No critical errors found in log."
    78	    else
    79	        echo "  [WARN] Found $ERRORS errors in log (check manually)."
    80	        grep -i "ERROR" .nextflow.log | grep -v "ErrorStrategy" | head -n 5
    81	    fi
    82	fi
    83	
    84	echo "=== Integration Test Complete ==="
    85	exit 0


===== ./main.nf =====
     1	#! /usr/bin/env nextflow
     2	
     3	nextflow.enable.dsl=2
     4	
     5	// Import Modules
     6	include { LOCATE_GENE } from './modules/locate_gene.nf'
     7	include { SPLIT_LOCI } from './modules/split_loci.nf'
     8	include { EXTRACT_FLANKING } from './modules/extract_flanking.nf'
     9	include { PREPARE_INITIAL_DB } from './modules/prepare_initial_db.nf'
    10	include { ITERATIVE_SEARCH } from './modules/iterative_search.nf'
    11	include { CLUSTER_REGIONS } from './modules/cluster_regions.nf'
    12	include { PREPARE_HOME_PROTEOME } from './modules/prepare_home.nf'
    13	include { PLOT_SYNTENY } from './modules/plot_synteny.nf'
    14	include { COMPUTE_TREE } from './modules/compute_tree.nf'
    15	include { ANNOTATE_GOI } from './modules/annotate_goi.nf'
    16	
    17	// New Modules
    18	include { STAGE_GENOMES } from './modules/stage_genomes.nf'
    19	include { ASSESS_GENOME_QUALITY } from './modules/assess_quality.nf'
    20	include { FETCH_QUERY_FROM_ID } from './modules/fetch_query.nf'
    21	include { RESOLVE_GENE_INPUT } from './modules/resolve_query.nf'
    22	include { PHYLO_SORT } from './modules/phylo_sort.nf'
    23	include { FETCH_RELATED_GENOMES } from './modules/fetch_related.nf'
    24	include { FETCH_HOME_GENOME } from './modules/fetch_home.nf'
    25	include { GENERATE_REPORT } from './modules/generate_report.nf'
    26	include { BORROW_ANNOTATIONS } from './modules/borrow_annotations.nf'
    27	include { NORMALIZE_QUERY } from './modules/normalize_query.nf'
    28	
    29	// ==============================================================================
    30	// ANSI Color Codes (Script-level variables)
    31	// ==============================================================================
    32	
    33	c_reset = "\033[0m"
    34	c_bold = "\033[1m"
    35	c_dim = "\033[2m"
    36	c_black = "\033[0;30m"
    37	c_red = "\033[0;31m"
    38	c_green = "\033[0;32m"
    39	c_yellow = "\033[0;33m"
    40	c_blue = "\033[0;34m"
    41	c_purple = "\033[0;35m"
    42	c_cyan = "\033[0;36m"
    43	c_white = "\033[0;37m"
    44	
    45	// ==============================================================================
    46	// Console UI helpers
    47	// ==============================================================================
    48	
    49	def uiRule() {
    50	    return "${c_blue}${'═' * 63}${c_reset}"
    51	}
    52	
    53	def uiStatus(String level, String task, String detail = '') {
    54	    def levelColors = [
    55	        'RUN ': c_blue,
    56	        'OK  ': c_green,
    57	        'INFO': c_cyan,
    58	        'WARN': c_yellow,
    59	        'SKIP': c_dim,
    60	        'FAIL': c_red
    61	    ]
    62	    def key = (level ?: 'INFO').padRight(4).substring(0, 4)
    63	    def levelColor = levelColors.get(key, c_white)
    64	    def taskCol = task ? "${c_white}${task.padRight(24)}${c_reset}" : ''
    65	    def detailCol = detail ?: ''
    66	    def prefix = "${levelColor}[${key}]${c_reset}"
    67	    if (taskCol && detailCol) {
    68	        log.info "${prefix} ${taskCol} ${detailCol}"
    69	    } else if (taskCol) {
    70	        log.info "${prefix} ${taskCol}"
    71	    } else if (detailCol) {
    72	        log.info "${prefix} ${detailCol}"
    73	    } else {
    74	        log.info prefix
    75	    }
    76	}
    77	
    78	def uiPhase(int idx, String title) {
    79	    log.info ""
    80	    log.info uiRule()
    81	    log.info "${c_white}Phase ${idx}${c_reset} ${c_dim}|${c_reset} ${c_cyan}${title}${c_reset}"
    82	    log.info uiRule()
    83	}
    84	
    85	def printHeader() {
    86	    log.info ""
    87	    log.info uiRule()
    88	    log.info "${c_cyan}${c_bold}SynTerra${c_reset} ${c_dim}v2.0${c_reset}"
    89	    log.info "${c_dim}Phylogenetically-informed syntenic ortholog discovery${c_reset}"
    90	    log.info uiRule()
    91	}
    92	
    93	def printParams() {
    94	    def query_display = params.gene ? new File(params.gene).name : params.query_id
    95	    def home_display = params.mode == 'easy' ? params.home_species : (params.home_genome ? new File(params.home_genome).name : 'N/A')
    96	    def target_display = params.target_species ?: 'auto (taxonomic search)'
    97	    
    98	    log.info uiRule()
    99	    log.info "${c_white}Run Configuration${c_reset}"
   100	    log.info uiRule()
   101	    log.info "${c_dim}Query Gene     ${c_reset} ${c_green}${query_display}${c_reset}"
   102	    log.info "${c_dim}Home Genome    ${c_reset} ${c_green}${home_display}${c_reset}"
   103	    log.info "${c_dim}Mode           ${c_reset} ${c_yellow}${params.mode}${c_reset}"
   104	    log.info "${c_dim}Target Species ${c_reset} ${c_cyan}${target_display}${c_reset}"
   105	    log.info "${c_dim}Flanking Genes ${c_reset} ${params.n_flanking_genes}"
   106	    log.info "${c_dim}MMseqs Sens.   ${c_reset} ${params.mmseqs_sensitivity}"
   107	    log.info "${c_dim}Output Dir     ${c_reset} ${params.outdir}"
   108	    log.info uiRule()
   109	}
   110	
   111	printHeader()
   112	printParams()
   113	
   114	workflow {
   115	    log.info ""
   116	    
   117	    // ========== INPUT VALIDATION ==========
   118	    
   119	    // Check if we have EITHER gene file OR query_id
   120	    if (!params.gene && !params.query_id) { 
   121	        log.error "${c_red}No query provided. Please provide either --gene or --query_id${c_reset}"
   122	        exit 1
   123	    }
   124	    
   125	    if (params.gene && params.query_id) {
   126	        uiStatus('WARN', 'INPUT', 'Both --gene and --query_id provided; using --gene')
   127	    }
   128	    
   129	    // Easy mode: home_species is now optional (auto-detected from UniProt/NCBI ID)
   130	    // Only required when --gene is a FASTA file
   131	    if (params.mode == 'easy') {
   132	        // Validation deferred — species can be auto-detected from ID
   133	    } else {
   134	        if (!params.home_genome) { 
   135	            log.error "${c_red}Pro mode requires --home_genome${c_reset}"
   136	            exit 1
   137	        }
   138	        
   139	        if (!file(params.home_genome).exists()) {
   140	            log.error "${c_red}Home genome not found: ${params.home_genome}${c_reset}"
   141	            exit 1
   142	        }
   143	    }
   144	    
   145	    // Only check file existence if --gene looks like a file path (not a UniProt/NCBI ID)
   146	    if (params.gene && !params.gene.matches('^[A-Za-z]{1,3}[_P]?[0-9].*') && !file(params.gene).exists()) {
   147	        log.error "${c_red}Gene file not found: ${params.gene}${c_reset}"
   148	        exit 1
   149	    }
   150	    
   151	    uiStatus('OK', 'INPUT', 'Validation passed')
   152	    
   153	    // Channel setup — depends on mode
   154	    if (params.mode == 'easy') {
   155	        // --- Easy mode: Resolve gene input (ID → FASTA + species) ---
   156	        def gene_input = params.gene ?: params.query_id
   157	        def species_override = params.home_species ?: ''
   158	        
   159	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'RESOLVE_QUERY', 'Easy mode: resolving query input')
   160	        RESOLVE_GENE_INPUT(gene_input, species_override)
   161	        
   162	        // Use resolved FASTA as query
   163	        raw_gene_ch = RESOLVE_GENE_INPUT.out.fasta
   164	        
   165	        // Get resolved species (auto-detected from ID, or user-provided)
   166	        resolved_species_ch = RESOLVE_GENE_INPUT.out.species.map { it.text.trim() }
   167	        
   168	        // Determine home species: user-provided takes priority, else auto-detected
   169	        home_species_ch = resolved_species_ch.map { resolved ->
   170	            def species = params.home_species ?: resolved
   171	            if (!species) {
   172	                log.error "${c_red}Could not detect species. Please provide --home_species${c_reset}"
   173	                exit 1
   174	            }
   175	            return species
   176	        }
   177	        
   178	        // Fetch home genome automatically for easy mode
   179	        FETCH_HOME_GENOME(home_species_ch)
   180	        home_genome_ch = FETCH_HOME_GENOME.out.genome
   181	        // Use GFF if available, otherwise mark as missing
   182	        home_gff_ch = FETCH_HOME_GENOME.out.gff.ifEmpty(file("NO_GFF"))
   183	        
   184	        // Fetch related genomes for easy mode
   185	        def max_genomes = (params.max_genomes == null ? 10 : params.max_genomes as Integer)
   186	        def target_species = params.target_species ?: ''
   187	        FETCH_RELATED_GENOMES(home_species_ch, max_genomes, target_species)
   188	        genomes_dir_ch = FETCH_RELATED_GENOMES.out.genomes_dir
   189	        species_map_ch = FETCH_RELATED_GENOMES.out.species_map
   190	        
   191	    } else {
   192	        // --- Pro mode: User provides files directly ---
   193	        
   194	        // 1. Query Setup
   195	        if (params.gene) {
   196	            raw_gene_ch = Channel.fromPath(params.gene)
   197	        } else {
   198	            def pro_species_override = params.home_species ?: ''
   199	            RESOLVE_GENE_INPUT(params.query_id, pro_species_override)
   200	            raw_gene_ch = RESOLVE_GENE_INPUT.out.fasta
   201	        }
   202	        
   203	        // 2. Home Genome Setup
   204	        if (!params.home_genome) {
   205	            log.error "${c_red}Pro mode requires --home_genome${c_reset}"
   206	            exit 1
   207	        }
   208	        
   209	        home_genome_ch = Channel.fromPath(params.home_genome)
   210	        
   211	        if (params.home_gff) {
   212	            home_gff_ch = Channel.fromPath(params.home_gff).first()
   213	        } else {
   214	            home_gff_ch = Channel.value(file("NO_GFF"))
   215	        }
   216	        
   217	        // 3. Target Genomes Setup
   218	        if (params.target_genomes) {
   219	            uiStatus('RUN ', 'STAGE_GENOMES', 'Loading target genomes list')
   220	            target_genomes_list = Channel.fromPath(params.target_genomes).collect()
   221	            
   222	            // Show count
   223	            target_genomes_list.view { genomes ->
   224	                "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'STAGE_GENOMES'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} staged ${genomes.size()} target genomes"
   225	            }
   226	            
   227	            STAGE_GENOMES(target_genomes_list)
   228	            genomes_dir_ch = STAGE_GENOMES.out.dir
   229	            species_map_ch = Channel.fromPath("NO_SPECIES_MAP")
   230	            
   231	        } else {
   232	            uiStatus('WARN', 'STAGE_GENOMES', 'No target genomes provided; running home-genome-only analysis')
   233	            genomes_dir_ch = Channel.empty()
   234	            species_map_ch = Channel.fromPath("NO_SPECIES_MAP")
   235	        }
   236	    }
   237	
   238	    // Normalize query to protein space (DNA queries are translated to best ORF)
   239	    // to keep downstream search/annotation behavior consistent.
   240	    NORMALIZE_QUERY(raw_gene_ch)
   241	    normalized_gene_ch = NORMALIZE_QUERY.out.fasta
   242	
   243	    // PHASE 1: Core Localization
   244	    uiPhase(1, 'Gene Localization in Home Genome')
   245	    
   246	    uiStatus('RUN ', 'LOCATE_GENE', 'Locating GOI in home genome')
   247	    LOCATE_GENE(normalized_gene_ch, home_genome_ch)
   248	    
   249	    // 4b. ANNOTATE GOI EXONS
   250	    // Uses hits from LOCATE_GENE to annotate individual exons of the GOI
   251	    // If GFF available: matches GOI to annotated gene and extracts CDS/exons
   252	    // If no GFF: uses tblastn hits to detect exon boundaries (splice sites, start/stop codons)
   253	    uiStatus('RUN ', 'ANNOTATE_GOI', 'Annotating GOI exons')
   254	    
   255	    // Determine query_id for name-based GFF matching
   256	    def effective_query_id = params.query_id ?: ''
   257	    
   258	    ANNOTATE_GOI(
   259	        normalized_gene_ch.first(),
   260	        home_genome_ch,
   261	        home_gff_ch,
   262	        LOCATE_GENE.out.blast_hits,
   263	        LOCATE_GENE.out.mmseqs_hits,
   264	        effective_query_id
   265	    )
   266	    
   267	    ANNOTATE_GOI.out.info.view { info ->
   268	        "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'ANNOTATE_GOI'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} GOI exon annotation complete"
   269	    }
   270	    
   271	    // 5. SPLIT LOCI
   272	    SPLIT_LOCI(LOCATE_GENE.out.bed)
   273	    
   274	    SPLIT_LOCI.out.beds.flatten().count().view { count ->
   275	        "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'SPLIT_LOCI'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} identified ${count} locus/loci"
   276	    }
   277	    
   278	    distinct_loci_ch = SPLIT_LOCI.out.beds.flatten()
   279	        .map { file -> tuple(file.baseName, file) }
   280	    
   281	    // Prepare effective home GFF (borrowed annotations + predictions) when targets exist
   282	    def has_targets = params.target_genomes || params.mode == 'easy'
   283	    if (has_targets) {
   284	        home_gff_ch.branch { gff ->
   285	            real: gff.name != 'NO_GFF'
   286	            missing: true
   287	        }.set { gff_status }
   288	
   289	        // Prepare Home Proteome (Run once, used for RBH + borrowing)
   290	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'PREPARE_HOME', 'Preparing home proteome database')
   291	        PREPARE_HOME_PROTEOME(home_genome_ch, home_gff_ch, LOCATE_GENE.out.bed.first())
   292	        home_proteome_db_ch = PREPARE_HOME_PROTEOME.out.db
   293	        PREPARE_HOME_PROTEOME.out.db.view { db ->
   294	            "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'PREPARE_HOME'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} home proteome ready"
   295	        }
   296	
   297	        // Borrow only when home genome has no usable GFF.
   298	        gff_status.real.view { gff ->
   299	            "${c_dim}[SKIP]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'BORROW_ANNOT'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} home GFF found (${gff.name})"
   300	        }
   301	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'BORROW_ANNOT', 'Checking targets for annotation borrowing when home GFF is missing')
   302	        BORROW_ANNOTATIONS(
   303	            home_genome_ch,
   304	            PREPARE_HOME_PROTEOME.out.faa,
   305	            genomes_dir_ch,
   306	            LOCATE_GENE.out.bed.first(),
   307	            params.n_flanking_genes,
   308	            gff_status.missing.map { true }
   309	        )
   310	        BORROW_ANNOTATIONS.out.gff.view { gff ->
   311	            "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'BORROW_ANNOT'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} borrowed annotations generated"
   312	        }
   313	
   314	        // Build effective GFF from all available sources:
   315	        // 1. User-provided / NCBI GFF (if present and real)
   316	        // 2. Prodigal-predicted GFF (when no annotation was available)
   317	        // 3. Borrowed annotations from annotated target genomes
   318	        fallback_gff_ch = PREPARE_HOME_PROTEOME.out.gff
   319	            .mix(BORROW_ANNOTATIONS.out.gff)
   320	            .collectFile(name: 'merged_home_annotations.gff')
   321	            .ifEmpty(file("NO_GFF"))
   322	        
   323	        effective_home_gff_ch = gff_status.real
   324	            .concat(
   325	                gff_status.missing.combine(fallback_gff_ch).map { it[1] }
   326	            )
   327	            .first()
   328	    } else {
   329	        effective_home_gff_ch = home_gff_ch
   330	    }
   331	
   332	    // 6. Extract Flanking Genes
   333	    uiStatus('RUN ', 'EXTRACT_FLANKING', "Extracting flanking genes (n=${params.n_flanking_genes})")
   334	    
   335	    EXTRACT_FLANKING(
   336	        distinct_loci_ch, 
   337	        effective_home_gff_ch, 
   338	        home_genome_ch,
   339	        params.n_flanking_genes,
   340	        params.min_flanking_size,
   341	        params.prefer_large_genes
   342	    )
   343	    
   344	    // 6b. CRITICAL FIX: Prepare Initial Database with GOI included
   345	    // Combine flanking genes with query gene for iterative search
   346	    uiStatus('RUN ', 'PREPARE_DB', 'Preparing initial database with query gene')
   347	    
   348	    PREPARE_INITIAL_DB(
   349	        EXTRACT_FLANKING.out.faa,
   350	        ANNOTATE_GOI.out.exons.first()  // .first() → value channel so it pairs with ALL loci
   351	    )
   352	        // Only run if we have targets
   353	    if (params.target_genomes || params.mode == 'easy') {
   354	        
   355	        uiPhase(2, 'Phylogenetic Ordering and Iterative Search')
   356	        
   357	        EXTRACT_FLANKING.out.bed
   358	            .map { locus_id, bed -> locus_id }
   359	            .combine(home_genome_ch)
   360	            .set { phylo_sort_inputs } // [locus_id, home_genome]
   361	
   362	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'PHYLO_SORT', 'Sorting genomes by phylogenetic distance')
   363	        
   364	        PHYLO_SORT(
   365	            phylo_sort_inputs,
   366	            genomes_dir_ch
   367	        )
   368	        
   369	        PHYLO_SORT.out.sorted_list.view { locus, sorted ->
   370	            "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'PHYLO_SORT'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} ordering complete for ${locus}"
   371	        }
   372	        
   373	        // 8. Iterative Search (FOR EACH LOCUS) - Using FIXED database with GOI
   374	        PREPARE_INITIAL_DB.out.db
   375	            .join(PHYLO_SORT.out.sorted_list) 
   376	            .set { iterative_search_inputs_partial } // [locus_id, initial_db, sorted_list]
   377	            
   378	        iterative_search_inputs_partial
   379	            .combine(genomes_dir_ch)
   380	            .set { iterative_search_inputs } // [locus_id, faa, sorted_list, genomes_dir]
   381	
   382	        // QC
   383	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'GENOME_QC', 'Assessing target genome quality')
   384	        
   385	        ASSESS_GENOME_QUALITY(genomes_dir_ch)
   386	        qc_summary_ch = ASSESS_GENOME_QUALITY.out.json
   387	
   388	        iterative_search_inputs
   389	            .combine(home_proteome_db_ch)
   390	            .set { iterative_search_final_inputs } // [locus_id, faa, sorted_list, genomes_dir, home_db]
   391	
   392	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'ITERATIVE_SEARCH', 'Running iterative phylogenetic search')
   393	        
   394	        ITERATIVE_SEARCH(
   395	            iterative_search_final_inputs.map { tuple(it[0], it[1]) }, // [locus, faa]
   396	            iterative_search_final_inputs.map { it[2] }, // sorted_list
   397	            iterative_search_final_inputs.map { it[3] }, // genomes_dir
   398	            iterative_search_final_inputs.map { it[4] }, // home_db
   399	            params.n_flanking_genes,
   400	            params.min_synteny_score,
   401	            params.mmseqs_sensitivity
   402	        )
   403	        
   404	        ITERATIVE_SEARCH.out.expanded_db.view { locus, db ->
   405	            "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'ITERATIVE_SEARCH'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} complete for ${locus}"
   406	        }
   407	
   408	        // PHASE 3: Region Identification & Augmented Search
   409	        uiPhase(3, 'Region Clustering')
   410	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'CLUSTER_REGIONS', 'Clustering genomic regions by synteny')
   411	        
   412	        ITERATIVE_SEARCH.out.hits
   413	            .join(EXTRACT_FLANKING.out.faa)
   414	            .join(EXTRACT_FLANKING.out.bed)
   415	            .map { locus_id, hits_dir, faa_file, bed_file ->
   416	                // Explode hits directory
   417	                def dir_file = new File(hits_dir.toString())
   418	                if (dir_file.exists() && dir_file.isDirectory()) {
   419	                    dir_file.listFiles()
   420	                        .findAll { it.name.endsWith(".m8") }
   421	                        .collect { hit_file ->
   422	                            def genome_name = hit_file.name.replace(".m8", "").replace("${locus_id}_", "")
   423	                            tuple(genome_name, faa_file, hit_file.toPath(), locus_id, bed_file)
   424	                        }
   425	                } else {
   426	                     [] 
   427	                }
   428	            }
   429	            .flatten()
   430	            .collate(5) // [genome_name, faa_file, hit_file, locus_id, bed_file]
   431	            .combine(genomes_dir_ch) // Combine with genomes_dir -> [..., genomes_dir]
   432	            .set { clustering_inputs }
   433	
   434	        CLUSTER_REGIONS(
   435	            clustering_inputs.map { tuple(it[0], it[1], it[2], it[5]) }, // [genome, payload, hit, genomes_dir]
   436	            clustering_inputs.map { tuple(it[3], it[4]) }, // [locus_id, synteny_bed]
   437	            params.n_flanking_genes,
   438	            params.min_synteny_score
   439	        )
   440	        CLUSTER_REGIONS.out.bed.count().view { count ->
   441	            "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'CLUSTER_REGIONS'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} generated ${count} clustered region set(s)"
   442	        }
   443	
   444	        // --- PHYLOGENY & PLOTTING ---
   445	        // Collect all proteins for tree: Flanking Genes + Discovered Genes (from Expanded DB or Regions?)
   446	        // Iterative Search output might be best source of all gene sequences found.
   447	        // expanded_db contains everything found so far.
   448	        // Let's use expanded_db per locus.
   449	        
   450	        uiPhase(4, 'Phylogenetics and Visualization')
   451	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'COMPUTE_TREE', 'Computing GOI phylogenetic trees')
   452	        
   453	        COMPUTE_TREE(
   454	            ITERATIVE_SEARCH.out.expanded_db
   455	        )
   456	        
   457	        COMPUTE_TREE.out.tree.view { locus, tree ->
   458	            "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'COMPUTE_TREE'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} tree computed for ${locus}"
   459	        }
   460	        
   461	        // PHASE 4: Miniprot-based Annotation (Replacing Augustus/HomologySearch)
   462	        // Collect Data for Plotting (ALL loci)
   463	        // Group GFFs and TSVs by locus_id using direct join (not collect+combine)
   464	        gffs_by_locus_ch = ITERATIVE_SEARCH.out.gff
   465	            .transpose()
   466	            .map { locus_id, gff -> tuple(locus_id, gff) }
   467	            .groupTuple()  // [locus_id, [gff1, gff2, ...]]
   468	
   469	        tsvs_by_locus_ch = ITERATIVE_SEARCH.out.homology
   470	            .transpose()
   471	            .map { locus_id, tsv -> tuple(locus_id, tsv) }
   472	            .groupTuple()  // [locus_id, [tsv1, tsv2, ...]]
   473	
   474	        cluster_by_locus_ch = CLUSTER_REGIONS.out.bed
   475	            .map { genome_name, payload, locus_id, bed -> tuple(locus_id, genome_name, bed) }
   476	            .groupTuple()  // produces [locus_id, [names], [beds]]
   477	
   478	        home_bed_by_locus_ch = EXTRACT_FLANKING.out.bed  // [locus_id, bed]
   479	        tree_by_locus_ch = COMPUTE_TREE.out.tree         // [locus_id, tree]
   480	
   481	        plot_inputs = home_bed_by_locus_ch
   482	            .join(cluster_by_locus_ch)                    // [locus_id, home_bed, names, beds]
   483	            .join(tree_by_locus_ch)                       // [locus_id, home_bed, names, beds, tree]
   484	            .join(gffs_by_locus_ch, remainder: true)      // [locus_id, home_bed, names, beds, tree, gffs_or_null]
   485	            .join(tsvs_by_locus_ch, remainder: true)      // [locus_id, home_bed, names, beds, tree, gffs_or_null, tsvs_or_null]
   486	            .map { entry ->
   487	                def locus_id = entry[0]
   488	                def home_bed = entry[1]
   489	                def names = entry[2]
   490	                def beds = entry[3]
   491	                def tree = entry[4]
   492	                def gffs = entry[5] ?: []
   493	                def tsvs = entry[6] ?: []
   494	                tuple(home_bed, names, beds, gffs, tsvs, tree)
   495	            }
   496	
   497	        plot_inputs.multiMap { item ->
   498	            home_bed: item[0]
   499	            target_names: item[1]
   500	            candidate_beds: item[2]
   501	            target_gffs: item[3]
   502	            homology_tsvs: item[4]
   503	            tree: item[5]
   504	        }.set { plot_inputs_split }
   505	
   506	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'PLOT_SYNTENY', 'Generating synteny visualizations')
   507	            
   508	        PLOT_SYNTENY(
   509	            plot_inputs_split.home_bed,   // home_bed
   510	            LOCATE_GENE.out.bed.first(),  // query_bed
   511	            effective_home_gff_ch,        // home_gff (user-provided or Prodigal-predicted)
   512	            plot_inputs_split.target_gffs,    // target_gffs
   513	            plot_inputs_split.target_names,   // target_names
   514	            plot_inputs_split.candidate_beds, // candidate_beds
   515	            plot_inputs_split.homology_tsvs,  // homology_tsvs
   516	            plot_inputs_split.tree,           // tree
   517	            species_map_ch                    // species_mapping.tsv
   518	        )
   519	        
   520	        PLOT_SYNTENY.out.plot.view { plot ->
   521	            "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'PLOT_SYNTENY'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} synteny visualization complete"
   522	        }
   523	        
   524	        // Final Reporting
   525	        uiPhase(5, 'Report Generation')
   526	        uiStatus('RUN ', 'GENERATE_REPORT', 'Generating comprehensive report')
   527	        
   528	        ITERATIVE_SEARCH.out.region_genes
   529	            .map { it[1] } 
   530	            .flatten()
   531	            .collect()
   532	            .ifEmpty([])
   533	            .set { collected_regions }
   534	            
   535	        ITERATIVE_SEARCH.out.hits
   536	            .map { it[1] } 
   537	            .collect()
   538	            .ifEmpty([])
   539	            .set { collected_hits }
   540	            
   541	        // No standalone augmented proteins - integrated into iterative search
   542	        collected_augmented = Channel.of([]).collect()
   543	        
   544	        GENERATE_REPORT(collected_regions, collected_hits, collected_augmented, qc_summary_ch)
   545	        
   546	        GENERATE_REPORT.out.report.view { report ->
   547	            "${c_green}[OK  ]${c_reset} ${c_white}${'GENERATE_REPORT'.padRight(24)}${c_reset} analysis report generated"
   548	        }
   549	    }
   550	}
   551	
   552	workflow.onComplete {
   553	    log.info ""
   554	    log.info uiRule()
   555	    if (workflow.success) {
   556	        uiStatus('OK  ', 'PIPELINE', 'Pipeline completed successfully')
   557	        log.info "${c_dim}Results Directory:${c_reset} ${c_cyan}${params.outdir}${c_reset}"
   558	        log.info "${c_dim}Duration:         ${c_reset} ${workflow.duration}"
   559	        log.info "${c_dim}Tasks Completed:  ${c_reset} ${workflow.stats.succeedCount}"
   560	        log.info uiRule()
   561	        log.info "${c_dim}Key outputs:${c_reset}"
   562	        log.info "${c_dim}- synterra_report.json          (analysis summary)${c_reset}"
   563	        log.info "${c_dim}- *_synteny_plot.html           (interactive visualization)${c_reset}"
   564	        log.info "${c_dim}- *_tree.nwk                    (GOI phylogeny)${c_reset}"
   565	        log.info "${c_dim}- regions/*.regions.bed         (candidate regions)${c_reset}"
   566	        log.info "${c_dim}- intermediate/                 (per-phase artifacts)${c_reset}"
   567	        log.info uiRule()
   568	    } else {
   569	        uiStatus('FAIL', 'PIPELINE', 'Pipeline execution failed')
   570	        log.info "${c_dim}Duration:${c_reset} ${workflow.duration}"
   571	        log.info "${c_dim}Error:   ${c_reset} ${c_red}${workflow.errorMessage}${c_reset}"
   572	        log.info uiRule()
   573	    }
   574	}


===== ./modules/annotate_goi.nf =====
     1	process ANNOTATE_GOI {
     2	    tag "annotate_goi"
     3	    label 'process_medium'
     4	    publishDir "${params.outdir}/intermediate/annotate_goi", mode: 'copy'
     5	    
     6	    input:
     7	    path query_gene
     8	    path home_genome
     9	    path home_gff
    10	    path blast_hits
    11	    path mmseqs_hits

    14	    output:

    17	    path "goi_info.json", emit: info

    20	    def gff_arg = home_gff.name != 'NO_GFF' ? home_gff : 'NO_GFF'

    22	    def mmseqs_arg = mmseqs_hits.name != 'NO_MMSEQS_HITS' ? "--mmseqs_hits ${mmseqs_hits}" : ""
    23	    def qid_arg = query_id ? "--query_id ${query_id}" : ""
    24	    """
    25	    annotate_goi_exons.py \\
    26	        --query ${query_gene} \\
    27	        --genome ${home_genome} \\
    28	        --gff ${gff_arg} \\
    29	        ${blast_arg} \\
    30	        ${mmseqs_arg} \\
    31	        ${qid_arg} \\
    32	        --gff_search_window ${params.gff_search_window} \\
    33	        --gap_search_window ${params.gap_search_window} \\
    34	        --gap_min_size ${params.gap_min_size} \\
    35	        --gap_evalue ${params.gap_evalue} \\
    36	        --gap_min_identity ${params.gap_min_identity} \\
    37	        --gap_min_alnlen ${params.gap_min_alnlen} \\
    38	        --gap_max_hits ${params.gap_max_hits} \\
    39	        --min_exon_query_cov ${params.min_exon_query_cov} \\
    40	        --min_exon_alnlen ${params.min_exon_alnlen} \\
    41	        --output_exons goi_exons.faa \\
    42	        --output_bed goi_annotation.bed \\
    43	        --output_info goi_info.json
    44	    """
    45	}


===== ./modules/assess_quality.nf =====
     1	process ASSESS_GENOME_QUALITY {
     2	    publishDir "${params.outdir}/qc", mode: 'copy'
     3	    
     4	    input:
     5	    path genomes_dir
     6	    
     7	    output:
     8	    path "genome_qc_summary.json", emit: json
     9	    
    10	    script:
    11	    """
    12	    echo "[" > genome_qc_summary.json
    13	    first=true
    14	    # Find files safely - follow symlinks
    15	    find -L ${genomes_dir} -maxdepth 1 -type f \\( -name "*.fna" -o -name "*.fasta" -o -name "*.fa" \\) > genome_files.txt
    16	    while read f; do
    17	        if [ "\$first" = "true" ]; then first=false; else echo "," >> genome_qc_summary.json; fi
    18	        assess_genome_quality.py --genome "\$f" --output tmp.json
    19	        cat tmp.json >> genome_qc_summary.json
    20	    done < genome_files.txt
    21	    echo "]" >> genome_qc_summary.json
    22	    """
    23	}

===== ./modules/cluster_regions.nf =====
     1	process CLUSTER_REGIONS {
     2	    tag "cluster_regions"
     3	    label 'process_medium'
     4	    errorStrategy 'ignore'
     5	    publishDir "${params.outdir}", mode: 'copy'
     6	
     7	    input:
     8	    tuple val(genome_name), val(payload), path(hits_file), path(genomes_dir)
     9	    tuple val(locus_id), path(synteny_bed)
    10	    val flanking_count
    11	    val min_score
    12	
    13	    output:
    14	    tuple val(genome_name), val(payload), val(locus_id), path("regions/${genome_name}.regions.bed"), emit: bed
    15	    tuple val(genome_name), path("regions/${genome_name}.scores.tsv"), emit: scores
    16	
    17	    script:
    18	    """
    19	    mkdir -p regions
    20	    
    21	    # Resolve genome file
    22	    target_genome=\$(find -L $genomes_dir -name "${genome_name}*" -type f | head -n 1)
    23	    
    24	    cluster_grs.py \\
    25	        --hits $hits_file \\
    26	        --synteny_bed $synteny_bed \\
    27	        --flanking_count $flanking_count \\
    28	        --genome "\$target_genome" \\
    29	        --output regions/${genome_name}.regions.bed \\
    30	        --min_score $min_score \\
    31	        --cluster_dist ${params.cluster_distance} \\
    32	        --weight_base ${params.synteny_weight_base} \\
    33	        --weight_consistency ${params.synteny_weight_consistency} \\
    34	        --weight_strand ${params.synteny_weight_strand}
    35	        
    36	    # Create simple scores output from BED regions
    37	    # BED lines start with chrom, extract all non-empty lines as scores
    38	    if [ -s regions/${genome_name}.regions.bed ]; then
    39	        cp regions/${genome_name}.regions.bed regions/${genome_name}.scores.tsv
    40	    else
    41	        touch regions/${genome_name}.scores.tsv
    42	    fi
    43	    """
    44	}


===== ./modules/compute_tree.nf =====
     1	process COMPUTE_TREE {
     2	    tag "tree"
     3	    label 'process_medium'
     4	    publishDir "${params.outdir}", mode: 'copy'
     5	
     6	    input:
     7	    tuple val(locus_id), path(fasta_files)
     8	
     9	    output:
    10	    tuple val(locus_id), path("*.nwk"), emit: tree
    11	
    12	    script:
    13	    """
    14	    # Concatenate all fasta files
    15	    cat ${fasta_files} > all_sequences.faa
    16	    
    17	    # CRITICAL: Filter to only GOI sequences, then deduplicate by sequence content
    18	    # Identical sequences from different query proteins should be collapsed
    19	    python3 -c "
    20	import sys
    21	from collections import OrderedDict
    22	
    23	# Pass 1: collect all GOI sequences
    24	entries = []  # list of (name, seq)
    25	current_name = None
    26	current_seq = []
    27	
    28	for line in open('all_sequences.faa'):
    29	    if line.startswith('>'):
    30	        if current_name and current_seq:
    31	            entries.append((current_name, ''.join(current_seq)))
    32	        current_name = line.strip()[1:]
    33	        current_seq = []
    34	    else:
    35	        current_seq.append(line.strip())
    36	if current_name and current_seq:
    37	    entries.append((current_name, ''.join(current_seq)))
    38	
    39	# Filter to GOI only
    40	goi_entries = [(n, s) for n, s in entries if 'GOI_' in n or 'GOI|' in n]
    41	
    42	# Pass 2: deduplicate by sequence content
    43	# For identical sequences, keep one representative name
    44	seq_to_name = OrderedDict()  # seq -> first name seen
    45	name_counts = {}  # handle name collisions
    46	
    47	for name, seq in goi_entries:
    48	    if seq in seq_to_name:
    49	        continue  # skip identical sequence
    50	    # Handle name collisions (same name, different sequence)
    51	    if name in name_counts:
    52	        name_counts[name] += 1
    53	        unique_name = name + '_var' + str(name_counts[name])
    54	    else:
    55	        name_counts[name] = 1
    56	        unique_name = name
    57	    seq_to_name[seq] = unique_name
    58	
    59	# Write deduplicated output
    60	for seq, name in seq_to_name.items():
    61	    sys.stdout.write('>' + name + chr(10))
    62	    # Write sequence in 80-char lines
    63	    for i in range(0, len(seq), 80):
    64	        sys.stdout.write(seq[i:i+80] + chr(10))
    65	
    66	print(f'Deduplicated: {len(goi_entries)} GOI entries -> {len(seq_to_name)} unique sequences', file=sys.stderr)
    67	" > goi_only.faa 2> goi_dedup.log
    68	    head -5 goi_dedup.log || true
    69	    
    70	    # Check if we have sequences
    71	    count=\$(grep -c '^>' goi_only.faa 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    72	    echo "Filtered to \$count GOI sequences for tree"
    73	    
    74	    if [ "\$count" -lt 3 ]; then
    75	        echo "Not enough GOI sequences (<3) for tree, creating placeholder"
    76	        echo "(GOI_placeholder:0.0);" > ${locus_id}_tree.nwk
    77	    else
    78	        compute_tree.py \\
    79	            --input goi_only.faa \\
    80	            --output ${locus_id}_tree.nwk \\
    81	            --threads ${task.cpus}
    82	    fi
    83	    """
    84	}


===== ./modules/extract_flanking.nf =====
     1	process EXTRACT_FLANKING {
     2	    tag "$bed"
     3	    label 'process_medium'
     4	    publishDir "${params.outdir}/intermediate/flanking", mode: 'copy'
     5	
     6	    input:
     7	    tuple val(locus_id), path(bed)
     8	    path gff
     9	    path genome
    10	    val n_flank

    12	    val prefer_large
    13	
    14	    output:
    15	    tuple val(locus_id), path("synteny_block_${locus_id}.bed"), emit: bed
    16	    tuple val(locus_id), path("flanking_proteins_${locus_id}.faa"), emit: faa
    17	
    18	    script:
    19	    """
    20	    # v3: GOI overlap re-injection + fallback pseudo-gene (force re-run)
    21	    extract_flanking_genes.py \\
    22	        --bed $bed \\
    23	        --gff $gff \\
    24	        --genome $genome \\
    25	        --n_flank $n_flank \\

    30	        --pred_keep_pct ${params.pred_keep_pct} \\

    32	        --out_faa flanking_proteins_${locus_id}.faa

    34	}

===== ./modules/generate_report.nf =====
     1	process GENERATE_REPORT {
     2	    publishDir "${params.outdir}", mode: 'copy'
     3	    
     4	    input:
     5	    path region_files
     6	    path hits_dirs
     7	    path augmented_genes
     8	    path qc_json
     9	    
    10	    output:
    11	    path "synterra_report.json", emit: report
    12	    
    13	    script:
    14	    """
    15	    mkdir -p results/regions results/hits results/augmented
    16	    
    17	    # Check if inputs exist
    18	    for d in ${hits_dirs}; do
    19	        if [ -d "\$d" ]; then
    20	            cp -r \$d/* results/hits/ 2>/dev/null || true
    21	        fi
    22	    done
    23	    
    24	    for f in ${augmented_genes}; do
    25	       if [ -f "\$f" ]; then
    26	           cp \$f results/augmented/ 2>/dev/null || true
    27	       fi
    28	    done
    29	    
    30	    for f in ${region_files}; do
    31	       if [ -f "\$f" ]; then
    32	           cp \$f results/regions/ 2>/dev/null || true
    33	       fi
    34	    done
    35	    
    36	    generate_report.py --results_dir results --qc_json ${qc_json} --output synterra_report.json
    37	    """
    38	}


===== ./modules/iterative_search.nf =====
     1	process ITERATIVE_SEARCH {
     2	    tag "iterative"
     3	    label 'process_long'
     4	
     5	    input:
     6	    tuple val(locus_id), path(initial_db)
     7	    path sorted_genomes_file
     8	    path genomes // List or Dir
     9	    path home_db // MMseqs DB directory
    10	    val flanking_genes
    11	    val min_score
    12	    val sensitivity
    13	
    14	    output:
    15	    tuple val(locus_id), path("iterative_results/expanded_db.faa"), emit: expanded_db
    16	    tuple val(locus_id), path("iterative_results/hits"), emit: hits, optional: true
    17	    tuple val(locus_id), path("iterative_results/regions/*.faa"), emit: region_genes, optional: true
    18	    tuple val(locus_id), path("iterative_results/regions/*.gff"), emit: gff, optional: true
    19	    tuple val(locus_id), path("iterative_results/regions/*.homology.tsv"), emit: homology, optional: true
    20	
    21	
    22	    script:
    23	    """
    24	    iterative_search_runner.py \\
    25	        --initial_db $initial_db \\
    26	        --sorted_genomes $sorted_genomes_file \\
    27	        --genomes_dir $genomes \\
    28	        --home_db_dir $home_db \\
    29	        --output_dir iterative_results \\
    30	        --threads ${task.cpus} \\
    31	        --mmseqs_sens ${sensitivity} \\
    32	        --evalue ${params.search_evalue} \\
    33	        --min_identity ${params.min_hit_identity} \\
    34	        --min_length ${params.min_hit_length} \\
    35	        --max_intron ${params.max_intron} \\
    36	        --cluster_dist ${params.cluster_distance} \\
    37	        --min_gene_identity ${params.min_gene_identity} \\
    38	        --region_padding ${params.region_padding} \\
    39	        --padding_min ${params.padding_min} \\
    40	        --padding_max ${params.padding_max} \\
    41	        --enable_smith_waterman ${params.enable_smith_waterman} \\
    42	        --sw_method ${params.sw_method} \\
    43	        --sw_min_score ${params.sw_min_score} \\
    44	        --sw_min_identity ${params.sw_min_identity} \\
    45	        --sw_timeout_seconds ${params.sw_timeout_seconds} \\
    46	        --aug_relaxed_evalue_mult ${params.aug_relaxed_evalue_mult} \\
    47	        --aug_relaxed_evalue_cap ${params.aug_relaxed_evalue_cap} \\
    48	        --aug_relaxed_parse_evalue_mult ${params.aug_relaxed_parse_evalue_mult} \\
    49	        --aug_relaxed_identity_factor ${params.aug_relaxed_identity_factor} \\
    50	        --aug_relaxed_identity_min ${params.aug_relaxed_identity_min} \\
    51	        --aug_relaxed_length_div ${params.aug_relaxed_length_div} \\
    52	        --aug_relaxed_length_min ${params.aug_relaxed_length_min} \\
    53	        --aug_dedup_bin_bp ${params.aug_dedup_bin_bp} \\
    54	        --gap_search_window ${params.gap_search_window} \\
    55	        --gap_min_size ${params.gap_min_size} \\
    56	        --gap_evalue ${params.gap_evalue} \\
    57	        --gap_min_identity ${params.gap_min_identity} \\
    58	        --gap_min_alnlen ${params.gap_min_alnlen} \\
    59	        --gap_max_hits ${params.gap_max_hits} \\
    60	        --min_exon_query_cov ${params.min_exon_query_cov} \\
    61	        --min_exon_alnlen ${params.min_exon_alnlen} \\
    62	        --prefix ${locus_id}
    63	    """
    64	}


===== ./modules/locate_gene.nf =====
     1	process LOCATE_GENE {
     2	    tag "$home_genome"
     3	    label 'process_medium'
     4	    publishDir "${params.outdir}/intermediate/locate_gene", mode: 'copy'
     5	
     6	    input:
     7	    path gene
     8	    path home_genome
     9	
    10	    output:
    11	    path "home_gene_location.bed", emit: bed
    12	    path "hits_blast.txt", emit: blast_hits
    13	    path "hits_mmseqs.m8", emit: mmseqs_hits
    14	
    15	    script:
    16	    """
    17	    # 1. MMseqs2 Search
    18	    # Check query type (Protein vs DNA) - look for amino acid-specific chars
    19	    # Exclude E (valid IUPAC nucleotide) to avoid false positives
    20	    is_prot=\$(grep -v "^>" $gene | head -c 200 | grep -q "[DFHIKLMPQRSVWY]" && echo "true" || echo "false")
    21	    
    22	    if [ "\$is_prot" = "true" ]; then
    23	        echo "Detected Protein Query"
    24	        SEARCH_TYPE=2 # translated
    25	        BLAST_CMD="tblastn"
    26	    else
    27	        echo "Detected Nucleotide Query"
    28	        SEARCH_TYPE=3 # nucleotide
    29	        BLAST_CMD="blastn"
    30	    fi
    31	
    32	    # MMSEQS
    33	    # Removed --mask-mode 0 (invalid)
    34	    mmseqs easy-search $gene $home_genome hits_mmseqs.m8 tmp \\
    35	        --search-type \$SEARCH_TYPE \\
    36	        --format-output "query,target,pident,alnlen,mismatch,gapopen,qstart,qend,tstart,tend,evalue,bits" \\
    37	        -s ${params.mmseqs_sensitivity} || echo "MMSeqs search failed or found no hits"
    38	
    39	    # 2. BLAST Search
    40	    makeblastdb -in $home_genome -dbtype nucl
    41	    
    42	    if [ "\$BLAST_CMD" = "tblastn" ]; then
    43	        tblastn -query $gene -db $home_genome -out hits_blast.txt -outfmt 6
    44	    else
    45	        blastn -query $gene -db $home_genome -out hits_blast.txt -outfmt 6
    46	    fi
    47	
    48	    # 3. Combine and convert to BED for merging
    49	    
    50	    # Process MMseqs results
    51	    touch mmseqs.bed
    52	    if [ -s hits_mmseqs.m8 ]; then
    53	        awk -v OFS="\\t" '{ 
    54	            s=\$9; e=\$10; 
    55	            if(s>e){t=s; s=e; e=t; str="-"} else {str="+"} 
    56	            print \$2, s-1, e, "mmseqs", \$11, str 
    57	        }' hits_mmseqs.m8 > mmseqs.bed
    58	    fi
    59	
    60	    # Process BLAST results
    61	    touch blast.bed
    62	    if [ -s hits_blast.txt ]; then
    63	        awk -v OFS="\\t" '{ 
    64	            s=\$9; e=\$10; 
    65	            if(s>e){t=s; s=e; e=t; str="-"} else {str="+"} 
    66	            print \$2, s-1, e, "blast", \$11, str 
    67	        }' hits_blast.txt > blast.bed
    68	    fi
    69	
    70	    # Run Python Merge Script
    71	    merge_hits.py --mmseqs mmseqs.bed --blast blast.bed --output home_gene_location.bed
    72	    """
    73	}

===== ./modules/phylo_sort.nf =====
     1	process PHYLO_SORT {
     2	    tag "sorting"
     3	    publishDir "${params.outdir}/intermediate/phylo_sort", mode: 'copy'
     4	    
     5	    input:
     6	    tuple val(locus_id), path(home_genome)
     7	    path genomes_dir // Directory instead of list
     8	    
     9	    output:
    10	    tuple val(locus_id), path("sorted_genomes.txt"), emit: sorted_list
    11	    
    12	    script:
    13	    """
    14	    # Try phylogenetic sorting if taxonomy database available
    15	    # Otherwise fall back to simple listing
    16	    
    17	    TAXDB=\${TAXDB:-NO_TAXDB}
    18	    HOME_NAME="${params.home_species ?: ''}"
    19	    if [ -n "\$HOME_NAME" ]; then
    20	        HOME_QUERY="\$HOME_NAME"
    21	    else
    22	        HOME_QUERY="$home_genome"
    23	    fi
    24	    
    25	    if [ -d "\$TAXDB" ] && [ -f "\$TAXDB/nodes.dmp" ]; then
    26	        echo "Attempting phylogenetic sorting using TaxDB: \$TAXDB"
    27	        phylo_sort.py \\
    28	            --home "\$HOME_QUERY" \\
    29	            --targets_dir $genomes_dir \\
    30	            --taxdb \$TAXDB \\
    31	            --output sorted_genomes.txt || {
    32	                echo "Phylogenetic sorting failed, falling back to alphabetical"
    33	                phylo_sort.py --home "\$HOME_QUERY" --targets_dir $genomes_dir --taxdb "NO_DB" --output sorted_genomes.txt
    34	            }
    35	    else
    36	        echo "No TaxDB found (set TAXDB environment variable)"
    37	        echo "Using alphabetical order (no phylogenetic sorting)"
    38	        # Use phylo_sort's fallback logic
    39	        phylo_sort.py --home "\$HOME_QUERY" --targets_dir $genomes_dir --taxdb "NO_DB" --output sorted_genomes.txt
    40	    fi
    41	    """
    42	}


===== ./modules/plot_synteny.nf =====
     1	process PLOT_SYNTENY {
     2	    publishDir "${params.outdir}", mode: 'copy'
     3	
     4	    input:
     5	    path home_bed
     6	    path query_bed
     7	    path home_gff
     8	    path target_gffs
     9	    val target_names
    10	    path candidate_beds
    11	    path homology_tsvs
    12	    path tree
    13	    path species_map
    14	
    15	    output:
    16	    path "*_synteny_plot.html", emit: plot
    17	    path "*_tree.html", emit: tree, optional: true
    18	    path "plot_inputs_*", emit: inputs, optional: true
    19	
    20	    script:
    21	    // Handle empty collections gracefully
    22	    def gffs_str = target_gffs ? target_gffs.join(' ') : ''
    23	    def names_str = target_names ? target_names.join(' ') : ''
    24	    def cands_str = candidate_beds ? candidate_beds.join(' ') : ''
    25	    def homo_str = homology_tsvs ? homology_tsvs.join(' ') : ''
    26	    
    27	    // Only include arguments if they have values
    28	    def gffs_arg = gffs_str ? "--target_gffs ${gffs_str}" : ""
    29	    def names_arg = names_str ? "--target_names ${names_str}" : ""
    30	    def cands_arg = cands_str ? "--candidate_beds ${cands_str}" : ""
    31	    def homo_arg = homo_str ? "--homology_tsvs ${homo_str}" : ""
    32	    def species_arg = species_map.name != 'NO_SPECIES_MAP' ? "--species_map ${species_map}" : ""
    33	    
    34	    """
    35	    inputs_dir="plot_inputs_${home_bed.baseName}"
    36	    mkdir -p "\$inputs_dir"
    37	    cp $home_bed "\$inputs_dir/" || true
    38	    cp $query_bed "\$inputs_dir/" || true
    39	    if [ "$home_gff" != "NO_GFF" ]; then
    40	        cp $home_gff "\$inputs_dir/" || true
    41	    fi
    42	    if [ -n "${gffs_str}" ]; then
    43	        cp ${gffs_str} "\$inputs_dir/" || true
    44	    fi
    45	    if [ -n "${cands_str}" ]; then
    46	        cp ${cands_str} "\$inputs_dir/" || true
    47	    fi
    48	    if [ -n "${homo_str}" ]; then
    49	        cp ${homo_str} "\$inputs_dir/" || true
    50	    fi
    51	    if [ "$tree" != "NO_TREE" ]; then
    52	        cp $tree "\$inputs_dir/" || true
    53	    fi
    54	    if [ "$species_map" != "NO_SPECIES_MAP" ]; then
    55	        cp $species_map "\$inputs_dir/" || true
    56	    fi
    57	
    58	    plot_synteny.py \\
    59	        --query_bed $query_bed \\
    60	        --home_bed $home_bed \\
    61	        --home_gff $home_gff \\
    62	        $gffs_arg \\
    63	        $names_arg \\
    64	        $cands_arg \\
    65	        $homo_arg \\
    66	        --tree $tree \\
    67	        $species_arg \\
    68	        --output ${home_bed.baseName}_synteny_plot.html
    69	    """
    70	}


===== ./modules/prepare_home.nf =====
     1	process PREPARE_HOME_PROTEOME {
     2	    tag "home_prot"
     3	    
     4	    input:
     5	    path home_genome
     6	    path home_gff
     7	    path goi_bed
     8	    
     9	    output:
    10	    path "home_proteome.faa", emit: faa
    11	    path "home_proteome_db", emit: db
    12	    path "home_predicted.gff", emit: gff, optional: true
    13	    
    14	    script:
    15	    """
    16	    if [ "$home_gff" != "NO_GFF" ]; then
    17	        echo "Extracting proteins from Home GFF..."
    18	        gff_to_faa.py --gff $home_gff --genome $home_genome --output home_proteome.faa
    19	        # No predicted GFF needed - use provided one
    20	        touch home_predicted.gff.skip
    21	    else
    22	        echo "No Home GFF provided. Predicting proteins with Prodigal (GOI regions only)..."
    23	        prodigal_on_regions.py \\
    24	            --genome $home_genome \\
    25	            --goi_bed $goi_bed \\
    26	            --window ${params.pred_flank_window} \\
    27	            --output_faa home_proteome.faa \\
    28	            --output_gff home_predicted.gff \\
    29	            --fallback_full_genome ${params.prodigal_full_genome_fallback}
    30	    fi
    31	    
    32	    # Create MMseqs DB for fast searching
    33	    mkdir home_proteome_db
    34	    mmseqs createdb home_proteome.faa home_proteome_db/db
    35	    """
    36	}


===== ./modules/prepare_initial_db.nf =====
     1	process PREPARE_INITIAL_DB {
     2	    tag "prepare_db_${locus_id}"
     3	    label 'process_low'
     4	    publishDir "${params.outdir}/intermediate/initial_db", mode: 'copy'
     5	
     6	    input:
     7	    tuple val(locus_id), path(flanking_faa)
     8	    path goi_exons  // From ANNOTATE_GOI: contains full GOI + individual exon sequences
     9	
    10	    output:
    11	    tuple val(locus_id), path("initial_db_${locus_id}.faa"), emit: db
    12	
    13	    script:
    14	    """
    15	    #!/usr/bin/env python3
    16	    
    17	    # Build the initial search database from:
    18	    # 1. Flanking genes (from GFF or Prodigal prediction)
    19	    # 2. GOI exon sequences (from ANNOTATE_GOI - real exons, not arbitrary fragments)
    20	    # 3. Fallback fragments (halves/thirds) only if no real exons were found
    21	    
    22	    import sys
    23	    import os
    24	    
    25	    sys.path.insert(0, "${projectDir}/bin")
    26	    from sequence_utils import parse_fasta, write_fasta
    27	    from flanking_query_utils import collapse_flanking_query_records
    28	    from fragment_query import generate_fragments
    29	    
    30	    output_faa = "initial_db_${locus_id}.faa"
    31	    all_records = []
    32	    
    33	    # 1. Add flanking genes (collapse split exon/fragment records to one query per gene)
    34	    print("Loading flanking genes from ${flanking_faa}...")
    35	    flanking_raw = list(parse_fasta("${flanking_faa}"))
    36	    flanking_records, flanking_stats = collapse_flanking_query_records(flanking_raw)
    37	    for clean_id, seq in flanking_records:
    38	        all_records.append((clean_id, seq))
    39	    flanking_count = len(all_records)
    40	    print(
    41	        "  Loaded {count} normalized flanking genes "
    42	        "(from {inp} records; exon_reconstructed={recon}, fragment_collapsed={frag}, dropped_empty={drop})"
    43	        .format(
    44	            count=flanking_count,
    45	            inp=flanking_stats.get("input_records", 0),
    46	            recon=flanking_stats.get("exon_reconstructed", 0),
    47	            frag=flanking_stats.get("fragment_collapsed", 0),
    48	            drop=flanking_stats.get("dropped_empty", 0),
    49	        )
    50	    )
    51	    
    52	    # 2. Add GOI sequences (full protein + individual exons from ANNOTATE_GOI)
    53	    print("Loading GOI exon sequences from ${goi_exons}...")
    54	    goi_records = list(parse_fasta("${goi_exons}"))
    55	    if not goi_records:
    56	        print("ERROR: No sequences found in GOI exons file!", file=sys.stderr)
    57	        sys.exit(1)
    58	    
    59	    goi_full_count = 0
    60	    exon_count = 0
    61	    tandem_count = 0
    62	    full_goi_seq = None
    63	    full_goi_id = None
    64	    
    65	    for header, clean_id, seq in goi_records:
    66	        all_records.append((clean_id, seq))
    67	        
    68	        if '|exon_' in clean_id:
    69	            exon_count += 1
    70	            print(f"  Added exon: {clean_id} ({len(seq)} aa)")
    71	        elif clean_id.startswith('GOI_copy_'):
    72	            tandem_count += 1
    73	            print(f"  Added tandem copy: {clean_id} ({len(seq)} aa)")
    74	        else:
    75	            goi_full_count += 1
    76	            full_goi_seq = seq
    77	            full_goi_id = clean_id
    78	            print(f"  Added full GOI: {clean_id} ({len(seq)} aa)")
    79	    
    80	    # 3. Fallback: Generate arbitrary fragments ONLY if no real exons or tandem copies found
    81	    fragment_count = 0
    82	    if exon_count == 0 and tandem_count == 0 and full_goi_seq:
    83	        print("  No real exons found, generating fallback fragments...")
    84	        min_fragment_size = 20  # amino acids
    85	        fragments = generate_fragments(full_goi_seq, full_goi_id, min_size=min_fragment_size)
    86	        
    87	        for frag_id, frag_seq, frag_desc in fragments:
    88	            if "fragment_type=full" not in frag_desc:
    89	                all_records.append((frag_id, frag_seq))
    90	                fragment_count += 1
    91	        
    92	        print(f"  Generated {fragment_count} fallback fragments")
    93	    elif exon_count > 0:
    94	        print(f"  Using {exon_count} real exon sequences (no arbitrary fragments needed)")
    95	    elif tandem_count > 0:
    96	        print(f"  Using {tandem_count} tandem copies as queries (no arbitrary fragments needed)")
    97	    
    98	    # 4. Write combined database
    99	    write_fasta(all_records, output_faa)
   100	    
   101	    print(f"\\nInitial database created: {output_faa}")
   102	    print(f"  Total sequences: {len(all_records)}")
   103	    print(f"  - Flanking genes: {flanking_count}")
   104	    print(f"  - GOI (full): {goi_full_count}")
   105	    print(f"  - GOI exons: {exon_count}")
   106	    print(f"  - GOI tandem copies: {tandem_count}")
   107	    print(f"  - Fallback fragments: {fragment_count}")
   108	    """
   109	}
