===== ./nextflow.config =====
     1	manifest {
     2	    name = 'SynTerra'
     3	    description = 'Synteny-guided gene finding across genomes using iterative search'
     4	    mainScript = 'main.nf'
     5	    version = '1.0.0'
     6	    nextflowVersion = '!>=22.10.1'
     7	}
     8	
     9	params {
    10	    // Input - Query (one of these required)
    11	    // Input - Query (one of these required)
    12	    gene = null           // Query ID (UniProt/NCBI) or path to FASTA
    13	    query_id = null       // (Legacy) UniProt ID - prefer using --gene
    14	    
    15	    // Mode selection
    16	    mode = 'easy'         // 'easy' or 'pro'
    17	    
    18	    // Easy mode - provide UniProt ID or FASTA + species
    19	    home_species = null   // Optional if gene is UniProt/NCBI ID. Required for FASTA files.
    20	    max_genomes = 0       // 0 = Auto-detect based on taxonomy (3 per level). Or specify count.
    21	    target_species = null  // Comma-separated species names (e.g., "Ooceraea biroi,Harpegnathos saltator")
    22	    
    23	    // Pro mode - provide your own files  
    24	    home_genome = null    // Path to home genome FASTA
    25	    home_gff = null       // Path to home genome GFF (optional)
    26	    target_genomes = null // Glob pattern for target genomes (e.g., "genomes/*.fna")
    27	    
    28	    // Synteny parameters
    29	    n_flanking_genes = 10
    30	    prefer_large_genes = true
    31	    min_flanking_size = 500
    32	    exon_level_search = true  // Search at exon level for better divergent gene detection
    33	    
    34	    // Search parameters
    35	    cluster_distance = 50000
    36	    min_synteny_score = 0.6  // 60% flanking gene conservation required
    37	    min_hit_identity = 40
    38	    min_hit_length = 100
    39	    search_evalue = 1e-5
    40	    max_intron = 20000
    41	    
    42	    // Smith-Waterman search (for GOI in regions)
    43	    enable_smith_waterman = true  // Use rigorous SW alignment for GOI
    44	    sw_method = "auto"  // auto, parasail, or ssearch36
    45	    sw_min_score = 50
    46	    sw_min_identity = 20.0
    47	    sw_timeout_seconds = 300
    48	    
    49	    // Augmentation / adaptive search
    50	    region_padding = 150000
    51	    padding_min = 50000
    52	    padding_max = 200000
    53	    aug_relaxed_evalue_mult = 1000
    54	    aug_relaxed_evalue_cap = 10.0
    55	    aug_relaxed_parse_evalue_mult = 10
    56	    aug_relaxed_identity_factor = 0.6
    57	    aug_relaxed_identity_min = 25.0
    58	    aug_relaxed_length_div = 2
    59	    aug_relaxed_length_min = 15
    60	    aug_dedup_bin_bp = 100
    61	    
    62	    // Sensitivity
    63	    mmseqs_sensitivity = 8.5
    64	    min_gene_identity = 30
    65	
    66	    // Unused (reserved for future wiring)
    67	    augustus_species = 'honeybee1'  // Change based on your organism
    68	    expand_db_threshold = 1e-10
    69	    diamond_sensitivity = "very-sensitive"
    70	    enable_splice_variants = true
    71	    enable_frameshifts = true
    72	    mutation_rate = 0.05
    73	    num_mutant_variants = 10
    74	
    75	    // Annotation windows and filters
    76	    gff_search_window = 100000
    77	    gap_search_window = 50000
    78	    gap_min_size = 10
    79	    gap_evalue = 10
    80	    gap_min_identity = 25.0
    81	    gap_min_alnlen = 10
    82	    gap_max_hits = 5
    83	    min_exon_query_cov = 0.25
    84	    min_exon_alnlen = 30
    85	
    86	    // Prodigal prediction windows
    87	    pred_flank_window = 50000
    88	    pred_keep_pct = 0.10
    89	    prodigal_full_genome_fallback = false
    90	
    91	    // Synteny scoring weights
    92	    synteny_weight_base = 0.4
    93	    synteny_weight_consistency = 0.3
    94	    synteny_weight_strand = 0.3
    95	    
    96	    // Output
    97	    outdir = 'results'
    98	    
    99	    // Advanced options
   100	    keep_intermediate = false  // Set to true for debugging
   101	    max_retries = 3
   102	}
   103	
   104	// Profiles
   105	profiles {
   106	    standard {
   107	        process.executor = 'local'
   108	        conda.enabled = true
   109	        process.conda = "${projectDir}/environment.yml"
   110	    }
   111	    
   112	    conda {
   113	        conda.enabled = true
   114	        docker.enabled = false
   115	        singularity.enabled = false
   116	        process.conda = "${projectDir}/environment.yml"
   117	    }
   118	    
   119	    docker {
   120	        docker.enabled = true
   121	        docker.runOptions = '-u $(id -u):$(id -g)'
   122	        conda.enabled = false
   123	        singularity.enabled = false
   124	    }
   125	    
   126	    singularity {
   127	        singularity.enabled = true
   128	        singularity.autoMounts = true
   129	        docker.enabled = false
   130	        conda.enabled = false
   131	    }
   132	    
   133	    slurm {
   134	        process.executor = 'slurm'
   135	        process.queue = 'normal'  // Change to your cluster's partition
   136	        process.clusterOptions = '--account=your_account'  // Optional: billing account
   137	        // Uncomment and adjust for your cluster:
   138	        // process.queue = 'gpu'  // or 'long', 'batch', etc.
   139	        // executor.queueSize = 50  // Max jobs to submit at once
   140	        // executor.submitRateLimit = '10/1min'  // Rate limit submissions
   141	    }
   142	    
   143	    // Combined profiles for common HPC setups
   144	    hpc_singularity {
   145	        process.executor = 'slurm'
   146	        singularity.enabled = true
   147	        singularity.autoMounts = true
   148	        singularity.cacheDir = "${HOME}/.singularity/cache"
   149	        docker.enabled = false
   150	        conda.enabled = false
   151	    }
   152	    
   153	    hpc_conda {
   154	        process.executor = 'slurm'
   155	        conda.enabled = true
   156	        conda.useMamba = true
   157	        docker.enabled = false
   158	        singularity.enabled = false
   159	        process.conda = "${projectDir}/environment.yml"
   160	    }
   161	    
   162	    test {
   163	        includeConfig 'conf/test.config'
   164	    }
   165	    test_melettin {
   166	        process.executor = 'local'
   167	        conda.enabled = true
   168	        process.conda = "${projectDir}/environment.yml"
   169	        includeConfig 'conf/test_melettin.config'
   170	    }
   171	    test_tetramorium {
   172	        includeConfig 'conf/test_tetramorium.config'
   173	    }
   174	}
   175	
   176	// Process-specific resource requirements
   177	process {
   178	    // Default
   179	    cpus = 1
   180	    memory = '2 GB'
   181	    time = '1h'
   182	    
   183	    errorStrategy = { task.exitStatus in [143,137,104,134,139,140] ? 'retry' : 'finish' }
   184	    maxRetries = 2
   185	    
   186	    withName: 'ITERATIVE_SEARCH' {
   187	        cpus = 4
   188	        memory = '8 GB'
   189	        time = '4h'
   190	    }
   191	    withName: 'COMPUTE_TREE' {
   192	        cpus = 2
   193	        memory = '4 GB'
   194	    }
   195	}
   196	
   197	// Container definitions (used when docker or singularity profile is active)
   198	// Single container with all tools - simpler than per-process containers
   199	process {
   200	    // BioContainers mulled container with mmseqs2, mafft, fasttree, prodigal, miniprot, bedtools
   201	    // For custom container, build with Dockerfile and push to registry
   202	    container = 'quay.io/biocontainers/mulled-v2-949aaaddebd054dc6bded102520daff6f0f93ce6:aa5e66741ab99c00e7f30adb3c6e9fee22c5f5f5-0'
   203	}
   204	
   205	// Conda settings
   206	conda {
   207	    createTimeout = '1 h'
   208	    useMamba = true  // Use mamba for faster env creation (if available)
   209	}


===== ./scripts/reproduce_annotation.py =====
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	import sys
     3	import os
     4	import shutil
     5	import argparse
     6	import subprocess
     7	import json
     8	import re
     9	from collections import defaultdict
    10	
    11	# Add bin to path
    12	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath("bin"))
    13	
    14	from iterative_search_runner import (
    15	    parse_hits,
    16	    identify_synteny_blocks,
    17	    process_region_block,
    18	    extract_base_gene_id,
    19	    deduplicate_flanking_models,
    20	    collapse_flanking_cds_to_gene_span
    21	)
    22	from sequence_utils import load_genome, parse_gff, parse_fasta, write_fasta
    23	from flanking_query_utils import collapse_flanking_query_records
    24	
    25	def write_bed(hits, output_file, chrom_offset=0, chrom_override=None):
    26	    """Write hits to BED format."""
    27	    with open(output_file, 'w') as f:
    28	        for hit in hits:
    29	            # Input hits are 1-based inclusive. BED needs 0-based half-open.
    30	            start = max(0, hit['start'] + chrom_offset - 1)
    31	            end = hit['end'] + chrom_offset
    32	            chrom = chrom_override or hit['chrom']
    33	            name = hit['query']
    34	            score = hit['bits'] if 'bits' in hit else 0
    35	            strand = hit['strand']
    36	            # BED: chrom, start, end, name, score, strand
    37	            f.write(f"{chrom}\t{start}\t{end}\t{name}\t{score}\t{strand}\n")
    38	
    39	def first_truth_chrom(truth_gff):
    40	    """Return first non-comment seqid/chrom from ground-truth GFF."""
    41	    if not truth_gff or not os.path.exists(truth_gff):
    42	        return None
    43	    with open(truth_gff) as f:
    44	        for line in f:
    45	            if not line.strip() or line.startswith('#'):
    46	                continue
    47	            parts = line.rstrip('\n').split('\t')
    48	            if len(parts) >= 1 and parts[0]:
    49	                return parts[0]
    50	    return None
    51	
    52	def build_query_fasta(args):
    53	    """
    54	    Resolve query FASTA path:
    55	    1) Use --queries if provided and exists.
    56	    2) Otherwise combine --goi_queries + --flanking_queries into one FASTA.
    57	    GOI records are prefixed with GOI_ so iterative logic can treat them specially.
    58	    """
    59	    if args.queries and os.path.exists(args.queries):
    60	        return args.queries
    61	
    62	    if not os.path.exists(args.goi_queries):
    63	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"GOI query file not found: {args.goi_queries}")
    64	    if not os.path.exists(args.flanking_queries):
    65	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Flanking query file not found: {args.flanking_queries}")
    66	
    67	    combined_queries = os.path.join(args.outdir, "queries_combined.fasta")
    68	    records = []
    69	    seen = set()
    70	    seen_goi_seq = set()
    71	
    72	    for _, clean_id, seq in parse_fasta(args.goi_queries):
    73	        goi_id = clean_id if clean_id.startswith("GOI_") else f"GOI_{clean_id}"
    74	        if seq in seen_goi_seq:
    75	            continue
    76	        seen_goi_seq.add(seq)
    77	        key = (goi_id, seq)
    78	        if key in seen:
    79	            continue
    80	        seen.add(key)
    81	        records.append((goi_id, seq))
    82	
    83	    flanking_raw = list(parse_fasta(args.flanking_queries))
    84	    flanking_records, flanking_stats = collapse_flanking_query_records(flanking_raw)
    85	    print(
    86	        "Normalized flanking queries: {count} genes from {inp} input records "
    87	        "(exon_reconstructed={recon}, fragment_collapsed={frag}, dropped_empty={drop})".format(
    88	            count=len(flanking_records),
    89	            inp=flanking_stats.get("input_records", 0),
    90	            recon=flanking_stats.get("exon_reconstructed", 0),
    91	            frag=flanking_stats.get("fragment_collapsed", 0),
    92	            drop=flanking_stats.get("dropped_empty", 0),
    93	        )
    94	    )
    95	
    96	    for clean_id, seq in flanking_records:
    97	        key = (clean_id, seq)
    98	        if key in seen:
    99	            continue
   100	        seen.add(key)
   101	        records.append((clean_id, seq))
   102	
   103	    write_fasta(records, combined_queries)
   104	    print(f"Built combined queries FASTA: {combined_queries} ({len(records)} sequences)")
   105	    return combined_queries
   106	
   107	def compare_annotations(predicted_hits, truth_gff, offset=0, tolerance=10):
   108	    """
   109	    Compare predicted hits (list of dicts) against ground truth GFF.
   110	    """
   111	    print(f"\n[Comparison] Comparing {len(predicted_hits)} predictions vs {truth_gff}...")
   112	    
   113	    # Load Truth
   114	    truth_genes = parse_gff(truth_gff)
   115	    truth_exons = [f for f in truth_genes if f['type'] == 'CDS']
   116	    
   117	    print(f"Ground Truth: {len(truth_exons)} CDS features")
   118	    
   119	    tp = 0
   120	    fp = 0
   121	    fn = 0
   122	    
   123	    matched_truth = set()
   124	    matched_pred = set()
   125	    
   126	    def _norm_chrom(chrom):
   127	        if chrom is None:
   128	            return ""
   129	        return str(chrom).replace("region_seq", "region").split('.')[0]
   130	
   131	    for i, t in enumerate(truth_exons):
   132	        match_found = False
   133	        t_chrom = t.get('chrom', t.get('seqid')) # Handle both
   134	        
   135	        for j, p in enumerate(predicted_hits):
   136	            # Adjust prediction to genomic coords
   137	            p_start = p['start'] + offset
   138	            p_end = p['end'] + offset
   139	            p_chrom = p.get('chrom', p.get('target', 'unknown'))
   140	            
   141	            # Check overlap or boundary match
   142	            # Relaxed chrom check: if p_chrom is 'region' (from extracted FASTA), assume it matches
   143	            chrom_match = (_norm_chrom(t_chrom) == _norm_chrom(p_chrom)) or (p_chrom == 'region') or (p_chrom == 'region_seq')
   144	            
   145	            if chrom_match and t['strand'] == p['strand']:
   146	                # Exact boundary match (with tolerance)
   147	                start_diff = abs(t['start'] - p_start)
   148	                end_diff = abs(t['end'] - p_end)
   149	                
   150	                # Or check substantial overlap
   151	                overlap_start = max(t['start'], p_start)
   152	                overlap_end = min(t['end'], p_end)
   153	                overlap_len = max(0, overlap_end - overlap_start)
   154	                t_len = t['end'] - t['start']
   155	                p_len = p_end - p_start
   156	                
   157	                if (start_diff <= tolerance and end_diff <= tolerance) or \
   158	                   (overlap_len > 0.8 * t_len and overlap_len > 0.8 * p_len):
   159	                    match_found = True
   160	                    matched_pred.add(j)
   161	                    # print(f"  Match: Truth {t['start']}-{t['end']} vs Pred {p_start}-{p_end}")
   162	                    break
   163	        
   164	        if match_found:
   165	            tp += 1
   166	            matched_truth.add(i)
   167	        else:
   168	            fn += 1
   169	            # Report missed item
   170	            t_len = t['end'] - t['start']
   171	            t_name = t.get('attributes', {}).get('name', 'unknown')
   172	            t_note = t.get('attributes', {}).get('note', '')
   173	            t_chrom = t.get('chrom', t.get('seqid'))
   174	            print(f"  [MISS] {t_name} ({t_note}) at {t_chrom}:{t['start']}-{t['end']} ({t['strand']}), len={t_len}bp")
   175	            
   176	    fp = len(predicted_hits) - len(matched_pred)
   177	    
   178	    # Report false positives
   179	    for j, p in enumerate(predicted_hits):
   180	        if j not in matched_pred:
   181	            p_name = p['query']
   182	            print(f"  [EXTRA] {p_name} at {p['chrom']}:{p['start']+offset}-{p['end']+offset} ({p['strand']})")
   183	    
   184	    precision = tp / (tp + fp) if (tp + fp) > 0 else 0
   185	    recall = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) > 0 else 0
   186	    f1 = 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall) if (precision + recall) > 0 else 0
   187	    
   188	    print(f"Precision: {precision:.2f}")
   189	    print(f"Recall:    {recall:.2f}")
   190	    print(f"F1 Score:  {f1:.2f}")
   191	    
   192	    return {
   193	        'precision': precision,
   194	        'recall': recall,
   195	        'f1': f1
   196	    }
   197	
   198	def main():
   199	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Reproduce Annotation Logic")
   200	    parser.add_argument("--genome", default="tests/ground_truth_test/input/genome.fasta")
   201	    parser.add_argument("--queries", default=None,
   202	                        help="Combined query FASTA. If omitted, GOI/flanking inputs are merged.")
   203	    parser.add_argument("--goi_queries", default="tests/ground_truth_test/input/GOI_query.fasta")
   204	    parser.add_argument("--flanking_queries", default="tests/ground_truth_test/input/flanking_genes.fasta")
   205	    parser.add_argument("--ground_truth", default="tests/ground_truth_test/ground_truth/ground_truth.gff")
   206	    parser.add_argument("--outdir", default="tests/ground_truth_test/output")
   207	    parser.add_argument("--offset", type=int, default=0, help="Genomic offset for coordinates")
   208	    
   209	    args = parser.parse_args()
   210	    
   211	    os.makedirs(args.outdir, exist_ok=True)
   212	    query_fasta = build_query_fasta(args)
   213	    truth_chrom = first_truth_chrom(args.ground_truth)
   214	    
   215	    # Mock args object for iterative_search_runner functions
   216	    class MockArgs:
   217	        def __init__(self):
   218	            self.output_dir = args.outdir
   219	            self.mmseqs_sens = 9.5 # Max sensitivity
   220	            self.evalue = 10.0
   221	            self.min_identity = 30
   222	            self.min_length = 5
   223	            self.max_intron = 20000
   224	            self.cluster_dist = 50000
   225	            self.region_padding = 20000
   226	            self.padding_min = 5000
   227	            self.padding_max = 50000
   228	            # Keep harness portable: many environments here lack parasail/ssearch36.
   229	            self.enable_smith_waterman = False
   230	            self.sw_method = "auto"
   231	            self.sw_min_score = 15
   232	            self.sw_min_identity = 20
   233	            self.sw_timeout_seconds = 300
   234	            self.aug_relaxed_evalue_mult = 2000000000.0
   235	            self.aug_relaxed_evalue_cap = 20000.0
   236	            self.aug_relaxed_parse_evalue_mult = 10
   237	            self.aug_relaxed_identity_factor = 0.6
   238	            self.aug_relaxed_identity_min = 25.0
   239	            self.aug_relaxed_length_div = 2
   240	            self.aug_relaxed_length_min = 2
   241	            self.aug_dedup_bin_bp = 100
   242	            self.gap_search_window = 50000
   243	            self.gap_min_size = 10
   244	            self.gap_evalue = 10.0
   245	            self.gap_min_identity = 25.0
   246	            self.gap_min_alnlen = 10
   247	            self.gap_max_hits = 5
   248	            self.min_gene_identity = 30
   249	            self.min_exon_query_cov = 0.4
   250	            self.min_exon_alnlen = 5
   251	
   252	    runner_args = MockArgs()
   253	    
   254	    # 1. MMseqs Search (Genome vs Queries)
   255	    print(f"Searching {args.genome} with {query_fasta}...")
   256	    hits_file = os.path.join(args.outdir, "initial_hits.m8")
   257	    tmp_dir = os.path.join(args.outdir, "tmp_mmseqs")
   258	    
   259	    # Clean up previous run
   260	    if os.path.exists(tmp_dir): shutil.rmtree(tmp_dir)
   261	    
   262	    cmd = [
   263	        "mmseqs", "easy-search",
   264	        query_fasta, args.genome, hits_file, tmp_dir,
   265	        "--search-type", "2",
   266	        "--threads", "4",
   267	        "-s", str(runner_args.mmseqs_sens),
   268	        "-e", str(runner_args.evalue),
   269	        "--format-output", "query,target,pident,alnlen,mismatch,gapopen,qstart,qend,tstart,tend,evalue,bits"
   270	    ]
   271	    
   272	    subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
   273	    
   274	    # 2. Parse Hits
   275	    hits = parse_hits(hits_file, runner_args.min_identity, runner_args.min_length, runner_args.evalue)
   276	    print(f"Found {len(hits)} initial hits")
   277	    
   278	    if not hits:
   279	        print("No hits found! Exiting.")
   280	        return
   281	
   282	    # 3. Identify Synteny Block
   283	    print(f"\n[Analysis] Analyzing {len(hits)} hits for synteny...")
   284	    
   285	    # --- GLOBAL EVALUATION ---
   286	    # Check if the raw hits (after basic parsing/filtering) contain the ground truth
   287	    # This checks if MMseqs FOUND them, regardless of whether the pipeline SELECTS them.
   288	    from copy import deepcopy
   289	    global_hits = deepcopy(hits)
   290	    
   291	    # Filter global hits by toxin names (for fairness)
   292	    toxin_names = set()
   293	    if os.path.exists(args.ground_truth):
   294	        with open(args.ground_truth) as f:
   295	            for line in f:
   296	                if "name=" in line:
   297	                    match = re.search(r'name=([^;]+)', line)
   298	                    if match:
   299	                        toxin_names.add(match.group(1))
   300	    
   301	    if toxin_names:
   302	        global_hits = [h for h in global_hits if any(n in h['query'] for n in toxin_names)]
   303	    
   304	    print(f"[Global Check] {len(global_hits)} toxin hits found by MMseqs (genome-wide).")
   305	    
   306	    if os.path.exists(args.ground_truth):
   307	        print("\n[Global Check] Comparison (Ignoring Region Selection):")
   308	        compare_annotations(global_hits, args.ground_truth, offset=args.offset)
   309	    # -------------------------
   310	
   311	    # 3. Identify Synteny Blocks
   312	    # Match pipeline intent: use flanking genes as anchors, then search GOI inside regions.
   313	    flanking_seed_hits = [h for h in hits if not h.get('query', '').startswith('GOI_')]
   314	    goi_seed_hits = [h for h in hits if h.get('query', '').startswith('GOI_')]
   315	    if flanking_seed_hits:
   316	        seed_hits = flanking_seed_hits
   317	        print(f"[Analysis] Using {len(flanking_seed_hits)} flanking-anchor hits for block seeding.")
   318	    elif goi_seed_hits:
   319	        seed_hits = goi_seed_hits
   320	        print(f"[Analysis] No flanking anchors found; using {len(goi_seed_hits)} GOI hits for block seeding.")
   321	    else:
   322	        seed_hits = hits
   323	    synteny_blocks = identify_synteny_blocks(seed_hits, max_intron=runner_args.max_intron, cluster_dist=runner_args.cluster_dist)
   324	    
   325	    if not synteny_blocks:
   326	        print("No valid synteny blocks found.")
   327	        return
   328	
   329	    print(f"[Analysis] Found {len(synteny_blocks)} synteny blocks.")
   330	
   331	    # Prepare DB sequences
   332	    db_sequences = {}
   333	    for h, i, s in parse_fasta(query_fasta):
   334	        db_sequences[i] = {'id': i, 'seq': s, 'header': h}
   335	        base = extract_base_gene_id(i)
   336	        if base != i:
   337	            db_sequences[base] = {'id': i, 'seq': s, 'header': h}
   338	
   339	    genome_seqs = load_genome(args.genome)
   340	    
   341	    # 4. Process Blocks
   342	    all_gff_lines = []
   343	    
   344	    # Filter global hits by toxin names FIRST to define the set of interesting toxins
   345	    toxin_names = set()
   346	    if os.path.exists(args.ground_truth):
   347	        with open(args.ground_truth) as f:
   348	            for line in f:
   349	                if "name=" in line:
   350	                    match = re.search(r'name=([^;]+)', line)
   351	                    if match:
   352	                        toxin_names.add(match.group(1))
   353	    print(f"Loaded {len(toxin_names)} toxin names from ground truth for filtering.")
   354	
   355	    for i, block in enumerate(synteny_blocks):
   356	        print(f"  Block {i+1}: {block['chrom']}:{block['start']}-{block['end']} ({block['genes_count']} genes)")
   357	        try:
   358	             # Call helper
   359	            _, valid_gff = process_region_block(i, block, hits, genome_seqs, db_sequences, "test_genome", runner_args, "test_run", 4)
   360	            all_gff_lines.extend(valid_gff)
   361	        except Exception as e:
   362	            print(f"Error processing block {i}: {e}")
   363	
   364	    # Match pipeline behavior: flanking duplicates (same parent protein ID) are collapsed.
   365	    _, all_gff_lines = deduplicate_flanking_models(
   366	        [],
   367	        all_gff_lines,
   368	        genome_name="test_genome",
   369	        locus_gap_bp=max(5000, int(runner_args.cluster_dist)),
   370	    )
   371	    all_gff_lines = collapse_flanking_cds_to_gene_span(all_gff_lines)
   372	        
   373	    # 5. Parse GFF lines to hits for comparison
   374	    final_hits = []
   375	    all_cds_hits = []
   376	    all_gene_hits = []
   377	    
   378	    for line in all_gff_lines:
   379	        parts = line.split('\t')
   380	        if len(parts) >= 9 and parts[2] in {'mRNA', 'CDS'}:
   381	            chrom = parts[0]
   382	            start = int(parts[3])
   383	            end = int(parts[4])
   384	            strand = parts[6]
   385	            attr = parts[8]
   386	            
   387	            # Extract ID or Name
   388	            name = "unknown"
   389	            if "Name=" in attr:
   390	                m = re.search(r'Name=([^;]+)', attr)
   391	                if m: name = m.group(1)
   392	            elif "ID=" in attr:
   393	                 m = re.search(r'ID=([^;]+)', attr)
   394	                 if m: name = m.group(1)
   395	            
   396	            if parts[2] == 'mRNA':
   397	                all_gene_hits.append({
   398	                    'chrom': chrom,
   399	                    'start': start,
   400	                    'end': end,
   401	                    'strand': strand,
   402	                    'query': name
   403	                })
   404	                continue
   405	
   406	            all_cds_hits.append({
   407	                'chrom': chrom,
   408	                'start': start,
   409	                'end': end,
   410	                'strand': strand,
   411	                'query': name
   412	            })
   413	
   414	            is_toxin = False
   415	            if toxin_names:
   416	                for t in toxin_names:
   417	                    if t in name:
   418	                        is_toxin = True
   419	                        break
   420	            else:
   421	                is_toxin = True 
   422	            
   423	            # Keep benchmark metrics focused on GOI predictions.
   424	            if is_toxin and name.startswith("GOI_"):
   425	                final_hits.append({
   426	                    'chrom': chrom,
   427	                    'start': start,
   428	                    'end': end,
   429	                    'strand': strand,
   430	                    'query': name
   431	                })
   432	
   433	    aug_hits = final_hits
   434	    print(f"Collected {len(aug_hits)} final predicted toxin hits across all blocks.")
   435	    print(f"Collected {len(all_cds_hits)} total CDS annotations (toxin + flanking).")
   436	    print(f"Collected {len(all_gene_hits)} total gene-span annotations (toxin + flanking).")
   437	    # 6. Output BED
   438	    bed_file = os.path.join(args.outdir, "predicted_annotations.bed")
   439	    write_bed(aug_hits, bed_file, chrom_offset=args.offset, chrom_override=truth_chrom)
   440	    print(f"Written predictions to {bed_file}")
   441	
   442	    all_bed_file = os.path.join(args.outdir, "predicted_annotations_all.bed")
   443	    write_bed(all_gene_hits, all_bed_file, chrom_offset=args.offset, chrom_override=truth_chrom)
   444	    print(f"Written all gene-span annotations to {all_bed_file}")
   445	
   446	    all_cds_bed_file = os.path.join(args.outdir, "predicted_annotations_all_cds.bed")
   447	    write_bed(all_cds_hits, all_cds_bed_file, chrom_offset=args.offset, chrom_override=truth_chrom)
   448	    print(f"Written all CDS annotations to {all_cds_bed_file}")
   449	    
   450	    # 7. Compare
   451	    if os.path.exists(args.ground_truth):
   452	        results = compare_annotations(aug_hits, args.ground_truth, offset=args.offset)
   453	        
   454	        # Save metrics
   455	        metrics_file = os.path.join(args.outdir, "metrics.json")
   456	        with open(metrics_file, 'w') as f:
   457	            json.dump(results, f, indent=2)
   458	    else:
   459	        print("Ground truth file not found, skipping comparison.")
   460	
   461	if __name__ == "__main__":
   462	    main()


===== ./slurm_submit.sh =====
     1	#!/bin/bash
     2	#SBATCH --job-name=synterra
     3	#SBATCH --output=synterra_%j.log
     4	#SBATCH --error=synterra_%j.err
     5	#SBATCH --time=24:00:00
     6	#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
     7	#SBATCH --mem=8G
     8	#SBATCH --partition=normal
     9	# Optional: #SBATCH --account=your_account
    10	
    11	# ============================================================
    12	# SynTerra SLURM Submission Script
    13	# ============================================================
    14	# This script submits the Nextflow controller job.
    15	# Nextflow will then submit individual tasks to SLURM.
    16	# ============================================================
    17	
    18	# Load required modules (adjust for your cluster)
    19	module load java/17 2>/dev/null || module load java/11 2>/dev/null || true
    20	module load singularity 2>/dev/null || true
    21	module load conda 2>/dev/null || true
    22	
    23	# Ensure Nextflow is available
    24	export PATH="${HOME}/bin:${PATH}"
    25	export NXF_HOME="${HOME}/.nextflow"
    26	
    27	# Work directory (use fast scratch if available)
    28	export NXF_WORK="${SCRATCH:-${PWD}}/work"
    29	mkdir -p "${NXF_WORK}"
    30	
    31	# Singularity cache (for container images)
    32	export NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR="${HOME}/.singularity/cache"
    33	mkdir -p "${NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR}"
    34	
    35	# ============================================================
    36	# EDIT THESE PARAMETERS FOR YOUR RUN
    37	# ============================================================
    38	
    39	QUERY_ID="P01501"                    # UniProt ID (or use --gene for FASTA)
    40	HOME_SPECIES="Apis mellifera"        # Species name for Easy Mode
    41	MAX_GENOMES=10                       # Number of related genomes to fetch
    42	OUTDIR="results/${SLURM_JOB_NAME}_${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
    43	
    44	# Choose profile: hpc_singularity (recommended) or hpc_conda
    45	PROFILE="hpc_singularity"
    46	
    47	# ============================================================
    48	# RUN PIPELINE
    49	# ============================================================
    50	
    51	echo "Starting SynTerra pipeline..."
    52	echo "Job ID: ${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
    53	echo "Query: ${QUERY_ID}"
    54	echo "Species: ${HOME_SPECIES}"
    55	echo "Output: ${OUTDIR}"
    56	echo "Profile: ${PROFILE}"
    57	echo "Work dir: ${NXF_WORK}"
    58	echo "=============================================="
    59	
    60	nextflow run main.nf \
    61	    -profile ${PROFILE} \
    62	    --query_id "${QUERY_ID}" \
    63	    --home_species "${HOME_SPECIES}" \
    64	    --max_genomes ${MAX_GENOMES} \
    65	    --outdir "${OUTDIR}" \
    66	    -work-dir "${NXF_WORK}" \
    67	    -resume
    68	
    69	EXIT_CODE=$?
    70	
    71	echo "=============================================="
    72	echo "Pipeline finished with exit code: ${EXIT_CODE}"
    73	echo "Results: ${OUTDIR}"
    74	
    75	# Optional: cleanup work directory on success
    76	# if [ ${EXIT_CODE} -eq 0 ]; then
    77	#     rm -rf "${NXF_WORK}"
    78	# fi
    79	
    80	exit ${EXIT_CODE}

===== ./test_runner.sh =====
     1	#!/bin/bash
     2	set -e
     3	
     4	# Create dummy input
     5	echo ">gene1" > test_db.faa
     6	echo "MKAILIVGLSLWKS" >> test_db.faa
     7	
     8	echo ">chrom1" > test_genome.fna
     9	# Create random sequence
    10	# Sequence generation loop follows
    11	# Generate random sequence using python
    12	python3 -c "import random; print(''.join(random.choices('ACGT', k=2000)) + 'ATGAAGGCCATCCTGATCGTGGGCCTGAGCCTGTGGAAGAGC' + ''.join(random.choices('ACGT', k=2000)))" >> test_genome.fna
    13	
    14	echo "$(pwd)/test_genome.fna" > sorted.txt
    15	
    16	# Create dummy home db
    17	mkdir -p test_home_db
    18	mmseqs createdb test_db.faa test_home_db/db
    19	
    20	# Run
    21	python3 bin/iterative_search_runner.py \
    22	    --initial_db test_db.faa \
    23	    --sorted_genomes sorted.txt \
    24	    --output_dir test_out \
    25	    --home_db_dir test_home_db \
    26	    --min_length 10 \
    27	    --evalue 10 \
    28	    --threads 1
    29	
    30	echo "Run finished"
    31	if [ -f test_out/hits/test_genome.fna.m8 ]; then
    32	    echo "Hits file created (probably empty)"
    33	else
    34	    echo "Hits file missing"
    35	fi


===== ./tests/test_coordinates.py =====
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""
     3	Integration tests for coordinate transformations in SynTerra.
     4	
     5	Tests the critical coordinate conversion logic:
     6	1. GFF3 (1-based, closed) to BED (0-based, half-open)
     7	2. Local region coordinates to global genomic coordinates
     8	3. Miniprot GFF shifting from extracted region to chromosome
     9	"""
    10	
    11	import sys
    12	import os
    13	
    14	# Add parent directory to path
    15	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
    16	
    17	
    18	def test_gff_to_bed_conversion():
    19	    """Test GFF3 to BED coordinate conversion."""
    20	    # GFF3: 1-based, closed interval [start, end]
    21	    # Example: Gene from position 1000 to 2000 (inclusive)
    22	    gff_start = 1000
    23	    gff_end = 2000
    24	    
    25	    # BED: 0-based, half-open interval [start, end)
    26	    # Correct conversion:
    27	    bed_start = gff_start - 1  # 999
    28	    bed_end = gff_end          # 2000 (unchanged)
    29	    
    30	    # Verify: BED interval should represent same bases
    31	    # GFF [1000, 2000] = bases 1000-2000 inclusive = 1001 bases
    32	    # BED [999, 2000) = bases 999-1999 (0-indexed) = positions 1000-2000 (1-indexed)
    33	    
    34	    assert bed_start == 999, f"Expected bed_start=999, got {bed_start}"
    35	    assert bed_end == 2000, f"Expected bed_end=2000, got {bed_end}"
    36	    
    37	    # Length check
    38	    gff_length = gff_end - gff_start + 1  # 1001 bases
    39	    bed_length = bed_end - bed_start      # 1001 bases
    40	    assert gff_length == bed_length, f"Length mismatch: GFF={gff_length}, BED={bed_length}"
    41	    
    42	    print("✓ GFF to BED conversion correct")
    43	
    44	
    45	def test_local_to_global_shift():
    46	    """Test shifting coordinates from local region to global genome."""
    47	    # Global region: chr1:100000-200000
    48	    window_start = 100000
    49	    window_end = 200000
    50	    
    51	    # Local coordinates (within extracted region): 5000-6000
    52	    local_start = 5000
    53	    local_end = 6000
    54	    
    55	    # Global coordinates should be:
    56	    global_start = window_start + local_start  # 105000
    57	    global_end = window_start + local_end      # 106000
    58	    
    59	    assert global_start == 105000, f"Expected global_start=105000, got {global_start}"
    60	    assert global_end == 106000, f"Expected global_end=106000, got {global_end}"
    61	    
    62	    # Verify length is preserved
    63	    local_length = local_end - local_start
    64	    global_length = global_end - global_start
    65	    assert local_length == global_length, "Length changed during shift!"
    66	    
    67	    print("✓ Local to global coordinate shift correct")
    68	
    69	
    70	def test_miniprot_gff_shift():
    71	    """Test Miniprot GFF shifting (combines GFF parsing + coordinate shift)."""
    72	    # Scenario: Extract region chr1:50000-150000 (window_start=50000)
    73	    # Miniprot finds gene at local positions 10000-12000 (in extracted FASTA)
    74	    # Should output GFF with global positions 60000-62000
    75	    
    76	    window_start = 50000
    77	    
    78	    # Miniprot GFF (local coordinates, 1-based)
    79	    miniprot_local_start = 10000  # GFF format
    80	    miniprot_local_end = 12000
    81	    
    82	    # Shift to global (still in GFF format, 1-based)
    83	    global_gff_start = miniprot_local_start + window_start  # 60000
    84	    global_gff_end = miniprot_local_end + window_start      # 62000
    85	    
    86	    assert global_gff_start == 60000, f"Expected 60000, got {global_gff_start}"
    87	    assert global_gff_end == 62000, f"Expected 62000, got {global_gff_end}"
    88	    
    89	    print("✓ Miniprot GFF coordinate shift correct")
    90	
    91	
    92	def test_sequence_extraction():
    93	    """Test sequence extraction with coordinate systems."""
    94	    # Python string/BioPython sequence (0-based)
    95	    # Example: "ATCGATCGATCG" (12 bases, indices 0-11)
    96	    seq = "ATCGATCGATCG"
    97	    
    98	    # BED coordinates [3, 9) should extract indices 3-8 = "GATCGA" (6 bases)
    99	    bed_start = 3
   100	    bed_end = 9
   101	    
   102	    extracted = seq[bed_start:bed_end]
   103	    
   104	    assert extracted == "GATCGA", f"Expected 'GATCGA', got '{extracted}'"
   105	    assert len(extracted) == 6, f"Expected length 6, got {len(extracted)}"
   106	    
   107	    # Corresponding GFF would be [4, 9] (1-based, closed)
   108	    # Which also represents 6 bases: positions 4,5,6,7,8,9
   109	    gff_start = bed_start + 1  # 4
   110	    gff_end = bed_end          # 9
   111	    gff_length = gff_end - gff_start + 1  # 6
   112	    
   113	    assert gff_length == len(extracted), "GFF and BED represent different lengths!"
   114	    
   115	    print("✓ Sequence extraction with BED coordinates correct")
   116	
   117	
   118	def test_exon_clustering():
   119	    """Test multi-exon gene clustering logic."""
   120	    # Gene with 3 exons, should be clustered together
   121	    exons = [
   122	        {'start': 1000, 'end': 1200},
   123	        {'start': 2000, 'end': 2300},
   124	        {'start': 3500, 'end': 3800}
   125	    ]
   126	    
   127	    # Check gaps
   128	    gap1 = exons[1]['start'] - exons[0]['end']  # 800 bp
   129	    gap2 = exons[2]['start'] - exons[1]['end']  # 1200 bp
   130	    
   131	    # With max_intron=20000, all should cluster
   132	    max_intron = 20000
   133	    assert gap1 < max_intron, "Gap1 should be less than max_intron"
   134	    assert gap2 < max_intron, "Gap2 should be less than max_intron"
   135	    
   136	    # Total span
   137	    span = exons[2]['end'] - exons[0]['start']  # 2800 bp
   138	    max_gene_span = 500000
   139	    assert span < max_gene_span, "Gene span should be reasonable"
   140	    
   141	    print("✓ Exon clustering logic correct")
   142	
   143	
   144	def test_coordinate_edge_cases():
   145	    """Test edge cases in coordinate conversions."""
   146	    # Edge case 1: Gene at start of chromosome (position 1 in GFF)
   147	    gff_start = 1
   148	    gff_end = 100
   149	    bed_start = gff_start - 1  # 0
   150	    bed_end = gff_end          # 100
   151	    
   152	    assert bed_start == 0, "Start of chromosome should be 0 in BED"
   153	    assert bed_end == 100, "End should be 100"
   154	    
   155	    # Edge case 2: Zero-length feature (not typical, but should handle)
   156	    # GFF [1000, 1000] = 1 base
   157	    # BED [999, 1000) = 1 base
   158	    gff_single = 1000
   159	    bed_start_single = gff_single - 1  # 999
   160	    bed_end_single = gff_single        # 1000
   161	    length = bed_end_single - bed_start_single  # 1
   162	    
   163	    assert length == 1, "Single-base feature should have length 1"
   164	    
   165	    print("✓ Edge cases handled correctly")
   166	
   167	
   168	def run_all_tests():
   169	    """Run all coordinate transformation tests."""
   170	    print("=" * 60)
   171	    print("Running SynTerra Coordinate Transformation Tests")
   172	    print("=" * 60)
   173	    
   174	    tests = [
   175	        test_gff_to_bed_conversion,
   176	        test_local_to_global_shift,
   177	        test_miniprot_gff_shift,
   178	        test_sequence_extraction,
   179	        test_exon_clustering,
   180	        test_coordinate_edge_cases
   181	    ]
   182	    
   183	    passed = 0
   184	    failed = 0
   185	    
   186	    for test in tests:
   187	        try:
   188	            print(f"\n{test.__name__}:")
   189	            test()
   190	            passed += 1
   191	        except AssertionError as e:
   192	            print(f"✗ FAILED: {e}")
   193	            failed += 1
   194	        except Exception as e:
   195	            print(f"✗ ERROR: {e}")
   196	            failed += 1
   197	    
   198	    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
   199	    print(f"Results: {passed} passed, {failed} failed")
   200	    print("=" * 60)
   201	    
   202	    return failed == 0
   203	
   204	
   205	if __name__ == "__main__":
   206	    success = run_all_tests()
   207	    sys.exit(0 if success else 1)


===== ./tests/test_core_functions.py =====
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""
     3	Basic test suite for SynTerra pipeline core functions.
     4	Tests critical functions for correctness.
     5	"""
     6	
     7	import unittest
     8	import sys
     9	import os
    10	import tempfile
    11	import shutil
    12	
    13	# Add bin directory to path
    14	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), '..', 'bin'))
    15	
    16	from iterative_search_runner import (
    17	    normalize_coordinates,
    18	    extract_base_gene_id,
    19	    parse_hits,
    20	    identify_best_synteny_block
    21	)
    22	from sequence_utils import parse_fasta, write_fasta, reverse_complement, translate, extract_id
    23	
    24	
    25	class TestCoordinateNormalization(unittest.TestCase):
    26	    """Test coordinate handling functions."""
    27	    
    28	    def test_normalize_coordinates_forward(self):
    29	        """Test that forward strand coordinates stay in order."""
    30	        start, end = normalize_coordinates(100, 200)
    31	        self.assertEqual(start, 100)
    32	        self.assertEqual(end, 200)
    33	    
    34	    def test_normalize_coordinates_reverse(self):
    35	        """Test that reverse strand coordinates get swapped."""
    36	        start, end = normalize_coordinates(200, 100)
    37	        self.assertEqual(start, 100)
    38	        self.assertEqual(end, 200)
    39	    
    40	    def test_normalize_coordinates_equal(self):
    41	        """Test edge case where start == end."""
    42	        start, end = normalize_coordinates(100, 100)
    43	        self.assertEqual(start, 100)
    44	        self.assertEqual(end, 100)
    45	
    46	
    47	class TestGeneIDExtraction(unittest.TestCase):
    48	    """Test gene ID parsing and extraction."""
    49	    
    50	    def test_extract_base_simple(self):
    51	        """Test simple gene ID without suffixes."""
    52	        result = extract_base_gene_id("gene-LOC726866")
    53	        self.assertEqual(result, "gene-LOC726866")
    54	    
    55	    def test_extract_base_with_exon(self):
    56	        """Test gene ID with exon suffix."""
    57	        result = extract_base_gene_id("gene-LOC726866|exon_1")
    58	        self.assertEqual(result, "gene-LOC726866")
    59	    
    60	    def test_extract_base_with_variant(self):
    61	        """Test gene ID with variant suffix."""
    62	        result = extract_base_gene_id("gene-LOC726866|var1")
    63	        self.assertEqual(result, "gene-LOC726866")
    64	    
    65	    def test_extract_base_with_genome(self):
    66	        """Test gene ID with genome suffix."""
    67	        result = extract_base_gene_id("gene-LOC726866|GCA_000001234_MP000001")
    68	        self.assertEqual(result, "gene-LOC726866")
    69	    
    70	    def test_extract_goi_prefix(self):
    71	        """Test GOI-prefixed gene ID."""
    72	        result = extract_base_gene_id("GOI_gene-LOC726866")
    73	        self.assertEqual(result, "GOI_gene-LOC726866")
    74	
    75	
    76	class TestFastaIO(unittest.TestCase):
    77	    """Test FASTA reading and writing."""
    78	    
    79	    def setUp(self):
    80	        """Create temporary directory for test files."""
    81	        self.test_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()
    82	    
    83	    def tearDown(self):
    84	        """Clean up temporary directory."""
    85	        shutil.rmtree(self.test_dir)
    86	    
    87	    def test_write_and_read_fasta(self):
    88	        """Test round-trip FASTA writing and reading."""
    89	        test_file = os.path.join(self.test_dir, "test.fasta")
    90	        test_seqs = [
    91	            ("seq1", "ATCGATCG"),
    92	            ("seq2", "GCTAGCTA"),
    93	            ("seq3_with_description", "AAAATTTTCCCCGGGG")
    94	        ]
    95	        
    96	        write_fasta(test_seqs, test_file)
    97	        self.assertTrue(os.path.exists(test_file))
    98	        
    99	        read_seqs = list(parse_fasta(test_file))
   100	        self.assertEqual(len(read_seqs), 3)
   101	        # parse_fasta returns (raw_header, clean_id, sequence)
   102	        self.assertEqual(read_seqs[0][1], "seq1")  # clean_id
   103	        self.assertEqual(read_seqs[0][2], "ATCGATCG")  # sequence
   104	        self.assertEqual(read_seqs[1][1], "seq2")
   105	        self.assertEqual(read_seqs[1][2], "GCTAGCTA")
   106	    
   107	    def test_fasta_with_line_wrapping(self):
   108	        """Test FASTA with line-wrapped sequences."""
   109	        test_file = os.path.join(self.test_dir, "wrapped.fasta")
   110	        
   111	        # Write FASTA with wrapped lines
   112	        with open(test_file, 'w') as f:
   113	            f.write(">seq1\n")
   114	            f.write("ATCGATCGATCG\n")
   115	            f.write("ATCGATCGATCG\n")  # Continuation
   116	            f.write(">seq2\n")
   117	            f.write("GCTA\n")
   118	        
   119	        seqs = list(parse_fasta(test_file))
   120	        self.assertEqual(len(seqs), 2)
   121	        # parse_fasta returns (raw_header, clean_id, sequence)
   122	        self.assertEqual(seqs[0][2], "ATCGATCGATCGATCGATCGATCG")  # sequence
   123	        self.assertEqual(seqs[1][2], "GCTA")
   124	
   125	    def test_extract_id_preserves_long_loc_gene_ids(self):
   126	        """Ensure long LOC-style IDs are not truncated by GenBank accession regex."""
   127	        self.assertEqual(extract_id("LOC143834063"), "LOC143834063")
   128	        self.assertEqual(extract_id("LOC143834063 some description"), "LOC143834063")
   129	
   130	    def test_extract_id_keeps_genbank_accession_behavior(self):
   131	        """Ensure canonical GenBank protein accessions are still extracted."""
   132	        self.assertEqual(extract_id("AAA12345.1 hypothetical protein"), "AAA12345.1")
   133	
   134	
   135	class TestSequenceOperations(unittest.TestCase):
   136	    """Test sequence manipulation functions."""
   137	    
   138	    def test_reverse_complement(self):
   139	        """Test reverse complement operation."""
   140	        seq = "ATCGATCG"
   141	        result = reverse_complement(seq)
   142	        self.assertEqual(result, "CGATCGAT")
   143	    
   144	    def test_reverse_complement_lowercase(self):
   145	        """Test reverse complement with lowercase input."""
   146	        seq = "atcgatcg"
   147	        result = reverse_complement(seq)
   148	        self.assertEqual(result.upper(), "CGATCGAT")
   149	    
   150	    def test_translate_simple(self):
   151	        """Test translation of simple codon."""
   152	        seq = "ATG"  # Methionine
   153	        result = translate(seq)
   154	        self.assertEqual(result, "M")
   155	    
   156	    def test_translate_stop_codon(self):
   157	        """Test translation with stop codon."""
   158	        seq = "ATGTAA"  # M + STOP
   159	        result = translate(seq)
   160	        self.assertIn("M", result)
   161	        # Stop codon should be translated to *
   162	        self.assertIn("*", result)
   163	
   164	
   165	class TestHitsFiltering(unittest.TestCase):
   166	    """Test hit parsing and filtering."""
   167	    
   168	    def setUp(self):
   169	        """Create temporary directory and test hits file."""
   170	        self.test_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()
   171	        self.hits_file = os.path.join(self.test_dir, "test_hits.m8")
   172	        
   173	        # Write test hits (MMseqs2 format)
   174	        # query, target, pident, alnlen, mismatch, gapopen, qstart, qend, tstart, tend, evalue, bits
   175	        with open(self.hits_file, 'w') as f:
   176	            # Good hit
   177	            f.write("query1\tchr1\t90.0\t100\t10\t0\t1\t100\t1000\t1100\t1e-50\t200\n")
   178	            # Low identity (should be filtered)
   179	            f.write("query2\tchr1\t30.0\t100\t70\t0\t1\t100\t2000\t2100\t1e-10\t50\n")
   180	            # Short alignment (should be filtered)
   181	            f.write("query3\tchr1\t90.0\t10\t1\t0\t1\t10\t3000\t3010\t1e-5\t20\n")
   182	            # High evalue (should be filtered)
   183	            f.write("query4\tchr1\t90.0\t100\t10\t0\t1\t100\t4000\t4100\t1.0\t10\n")
   184	            # Another good hit
   185	            f.write("query5\tchr2\t85.0\t150\t22\t0\t1\t150\t5000\t5150\t1e-60\t250\n")
   186	    
   187	    def tearDown(self):
   188	        """Clean up temporary directory."""
   189	        shutil.rmtree(self.test_dir)
   190	    
   191	    def test_parse_hits_filtering(self):
   192	        """Test that hits are correctly filtered."""
   193	        hits = parse_hits(self.hits_file, min_identity=40.0, min_length=50, evalue_thresh=1e-5)
   194	        
   195	        # Should have 2 good hits (query1 and query5)
   196	        self.assertEqual(len(hits), 2)
   197	        self.assertEqual(hits[0]['query'], 'query1')
   198	        self.assertEqual(hits[1]['query'], 'query5')
   199	    
   200	    def test_parse_hits_strict_filtering(self):
   201	        """Test stricter filtering parameters."""
   202	        hits = parse_hits(self.hits_file, min_identity=87.0, min_length=100, evalue_thresh=1e-20)
   203	        
   204	        # Should have only 1 hit (query1)
   205	        self.assertEqual(len(hits), 1)
   206	        self.assertEqual(hits[0]['query'], 'query1')
   207	    
   208	    def test_parse_nonexistent_file(self):
   209	        """Test handling of nonexistent file."""
   210	        hits = parse_hits("nonexistent.m8", min_identity=40.0, min_length=50, evalue_thresh=1e-5)
   211	        self.assertEqual(len(hits), 0)
   212	
   213	
   214	class TestSyntenyIdentification(unittest.TestCase):
   215	    """Test synteny block identification."""
   216	    
   217	    def test_single_cluster(self):
   218	        """Test identification with hits forming single cluster."""
   219	        hits = [
   220	            {'query': 'gene1', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 1000, 'end': 1100},
   221	            {'query': 'gene2', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 1200, 'end': 1300},
   222	            {'query': 'gene3', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 1400, 'end': 1500},
   223	        ]
   224	        
   225	        region = identify_best_synteny_block(hits, cluster_dist=500)
   226	        
   227	        self.assertIsNotNone(region)
   228	        self.assertEqual(region['chrom'], 'chr1')
   229	        self.assertEqual(region['genes_count'], 3)
   230	        self.assertEqual(region['start'], 1000)
   231	        self.assertEqual(region['end'], 1500)
   232	    
   233	    def test_multiple_clusters_choose_best(self):
   234	        """Test choosing best cluster when multiple exist."""
   235	        hits = [
   236	            # Cluster 1: 2 genes
   237	            {'query': 'gene1', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 1000, 'end': 1100},
   238	            {'query': 'gene2', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 1200, 'end': 1300},
   239	            # Cluster 2: 3 genes (should win)
   240	            {'query': 'gene3', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 10000, 'end': 10100},
   241	            {'query': 'gene4', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 10200, 'end': 10300},
   242	            {'query': 'gene5', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 10400, 'end': 10500},
   243	        ]
   244	        
   245	        region = identify_best_synteny_block(hits, cluster_dist=500)
   246	        
   247	        self.assertIsNotNone(region)
   248	        self.assertEqual(region['genes_count'], 3)
   249	        self.assertEqual(region['start'], 10000)
   250	        self.assertEqual(region['end'], 10500)
   251	    
   252	    def test_no_hits(self):
   253	        """Test handling of empty hits list."""
   254	        region = identify_best_synteny_block([], cluster_dist=500)
   255	        self.assertIsNone(region)
   256	    
   257	    def test_different_chromosomes(self):
   258	        """Test hits on different chromosomes."""
   259	        hits = [
   260	            {'query': 'gene1', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 1000, 'end': 1100},
   261	            {'query': 'gene2', 'chrom': 'chr2', 'start': 2000, 'end': 2100},
   262	            {'query': 'gene3', 'chrom': 'chr1', 'start': 1200, 'end': 1300},
   263	        ]
   264	        
   265	        region = identify_best_synteny_block(hits, cluster_dist=500)
   266	        
   267	        # Should pick chr1 cluster (2 genes)
   268	        self.assertIsNotNone(region)
   269	        self.assertEqual(region['chrom'], 'chr1')
   270	        self.assertEqual(region['genes_count'], 2)
   271	
   272	
   273	def run_tests():
   274	    """Run all tests and return results."""
   275	    loader = unittest.TestLoader()
   276	    suite = unittest.TestSuite()
   277	    
   278	    # Add all test classes
   279	    suite.addTests(loader.loadTestsFromTestCase(TestCoordinateNormalization))
   280	    suite.addTests(loader.loadTestsFromTestCase(TestGeneIDExtraction))
   281	    suite.addTests(loader.loadTestsFromTestCase(TestFastaIO))
   282	    suite.addTests(loader.loadTestsFromTestCase(TestSequenceOperations))
   283	    suite.addTests(loader.loadTestsFromTestCase(TestHitsFiltering))
   284	    suite.addTests(loader.loadTestsFromTestCase(TestSyntenyIdentification))
   285	    
   286	    runner = unittest.TextTestRunner(verbosity=2)
   287	    result = runner.run(suite)
   288	    
   289	    return result
   290	
   291	
   292	if __name__ == '__main__':
   293	    result = run_tests()
   294	    sys.exit(0 if result.wasSuccessful() else 1)


===== ./tests/test_rbh_batch.py =====
     1	
     2	import unittest
     3	import sys
     4	import os
     5	import shutil
     6	import tempfile
     7	from Bio.SeqRecord import SeqRecord
     8	from Bio.Seq import Seq
     9	
    10	# Add bin to path
    11	sys.path.append(os.path.abspath("bin"))
    12	from iterative_search_runner import batch_rbh_check
    13	
    14	class TestRBH(unittest.TestCase):
    15	    def setUp(self):
    16	        self.test_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()
    17	        self.home_fa = os.path.join(self.test_dir, "home.faa")
    18	        self.home_db = os.path.join(self.test_dir, "home_db")
    19	        
    20	        # Create Home DB with GeneA and GeneX
    21	        with open(self.home_fa, 'w') as f:
    22	            f.write(">GeneA\nMKLLLL\n")
    23	            f.write(">GeneX\nMKVVVV\n")
    24	            
    25	        os.system(f"mmseqs createdb {self.home_fa} {self.home_db}")
    26	        
    27	    def tearDown(self):
    28	        shutil.rmtree(self.test_dir)
    29	        
    30	    def test_rbh_filtering(self):
    31	        # Case 1: GeneA -> hits GeneA (Valid)
    32	        rec1 = SeqRecord(Seq("MKLLLL"), id="GeneA|Variant1", description="")
    33	        
    34	        # Case 2: GeneB -> hits GeneX (Invalid, mismatch)
    35	        # Note: GeneB expects to find GeneB. But best hit is GeneX (seq match).
    36	        rec2 = SeqRecord(Seq("MKVVVV"), id="GeneB|Variant1", description="")
    37	        
    38	        candidates = [rec1, rec2]
    39	        unique_map = {
    40	            "GeneA|Variant1": "GeneA",
    41	            "GeneB|Variant1": "GeneB"
    42	        }
    43	        
    44	        valid = batch_rbh_check(candidates, self.home_db, unique_map)
    45	        
    46	        print(f"Valid IDs: {valid}")
    47	        self.assertIn("GeneA|Variant1", valid)
    48	        self.assertNotIn("GeneB|Variant1", valid)
    49	
    50	if __name__ == '__main__':
    51	    unittest.main()


===== ./tests/test_strand.py =====
     1	
     2	import unittest
     3	import sys
     4	import os
     5	import tempfile
     6	import shutil
     7	import subprocess
     8	
     9	class TestClusterGRS(unittest.TestCase):
    10	    def setUp(self):
    11	        self.test_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()
    12	        self.script = os.path.abspath("bin/cluster_grs.py")
    13	        
    14	    def tearDown(self):
    15	        shutil.rmtree(self.test_dir)
    16	        
    17	    def create_inputs(self, hits_data, bed_data):
    18	        hits_file = os.path.join(self.test_dir, "hits.m8")
    19	        bed_file = os.path.join(self.test_dir, "synteny.bed")
    20	        out_file = os.path.join(self.test_dir, "out.bed")
    21	        
    22	        with open(hits_file, 'w') as f:
    23	            for h in hits_data:
    24	                # query, target, pident, alnlen, mismatch, gapopen, qstart, qend, tstart, tend, evalue, bits
    25	                f.write("\t".join(map(str, h)) + "\n")
    26	                
    27	        with open(bed_file, 'w') as f:
    28	            for b in bed_data:
    29	                # chr, start, end, name, score, strand
    30	                f.write("\t".join(map(str, b)) + "\n")
    31	                
    32	        return hits_file, bed_file, out_file
    33	
    34	    def run_script(self, hits, bed, out):
    35	        cmd = [
    36	            sys.executable, self.script,
    37	            "--hits", hits,
    38	            "--synteny_bed", bed,
    39	            "--flanking_count", "3",
    40	            "--output", out,
    41	            "--min_score", "0.1" 
    42	        ]
    43	        result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    44	        return result
    45	
    46	    def test_perfect_synteny(self):
    47	        # Home: A(+) B(+) C(+)
    48	        bed = [
    49	            ["c1", 100, 200, "A", 0, "+"],
    50	            ["c1", 300, 400, "B", 0, "+"],
    51	            ["c1", 500, 600, "C", 0, "+"]
    52	        ]
    53	        # Target: A(+) B(+) C(+)
    54	        # Hits: tstart < tend for + strand
    55	        hits = [
    56	            ["A", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1000, 1100, 0, 100],
    57	            ["B", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1200, 1300, 0, 100],
    58	            ["C", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1400, 1500, 0, 100]
    59	        ]
    60	        
    61	        h, b, o = self.create_inputs(hits, bed)
    62	        res = self.run_script(h, b, o)
    63	        
    64	        print("Perfect Output:", res.stdout)
    65	        self.assertIn("score: 1.00", res.stdout.lower())
    66	        self.assertIn("consistency: 1.00", res.stdout.lower())
    67	        self.assertIn("strand: 1.00", res.stdout.lower())
    68	
    69	    def test_inverted_synteny(self):
    70	        # Home: A(+) B(+) C(+)
    71	        bed = [
    72	            ["c1", 100, 200, "A", 0, "+"],
    73	            ["c1", 300, 400, "B", 0, "+"],
    74	            ["c1", 500, 600, "C", 0, "+"]
    75	        ]
    76	        # Target: C(-) B(-) A(-)
    77	        # Hits: tstart > tend for - strand
    78	        # Order on genome: A(3000) B(2000) C(1000)? No.
    79	        # Genome: ... C ... B ... A ... 
    80	        # C is at 1000, B at 1200, A at 1400 ?? 
    81	        # If Inverted: A should be at HIGH coord, C at LOW coord?
    82	        # A, B, C order in query.
    83	        # Inverted Target: C, B, A order.
    84	        # C at 1000, B at 1200, A at 1400.
    85	        # But strands are minus.
    86	        
    87	        hits = [
    88	            ["C", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1100, 1000, 0, 100], # - strand
    89	            ["B", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1300, 1200, 0, 100], # - strand
    90	            ["A", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1500, 1400, 0, 100]  # - strand
    91	        ]
    92	        
    93	        h, b, o = self.create_inputs(hits, bed)
    94	        res = self.run_script(h, b, o)
    95	        
    96	        print("Inverted Output:", res.stdout)
    97	        # Unique: 3/3 (1.0)
    98	        # Consistency: 
    99	        # Order indices: C(2) -> B(1) -> A(0).
   100	        # Diffs: |2-1|=1, |1-0|=1. Consistent!
   101	        # Strand: All (-). Expected (+). Ratio 0/3 Same?
   102	        # But Inverted logic: We take MAX(Same, Diff).
   103	        # Diff = 3/3 = 1.0.
   104	        # Should be perfect score.
   105	        
   106	        self.assertIn("score: 1.00", res.stdout.lower())
   107	        
   108	    def test_bad_strand(self):
   109	        # Home: A(+) B(+) C(+)
   110	        bed = [
   111	            ["c1", 100, 200, "A", 0, "+"],
   112	            ["c1", 300, 400, "B", 0, "+"],
   113	            ["c1", 500, 600, "C", 0, "+"]
   114	        ]
   115	        # Target: Mixed
   116	        hits = [
   117	            ["A", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1000, 1100, 0, 100], # +
   118	            ["B", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1300, 1200, 0, 100], # - (BAD)
   119	            ["C", "t1", 100, 100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1400, 1500, 0, 100]  # +
   120	        ]
   121	        
   122	        h, b, o = self.create_inputs(hits, bed)
   123	        res = self.run_script(h, b, o)
   124	        
   125	        print("Mixed Output:", res.stdout)
   126	        # Strand cons: 2 same, 1 diff.
   127	        # Score should be lower than 1.0.
   128	        
   129	        # Verify score is < 1.0
   130	        # Expecting something like 0.8 or 0.7
   131	        self.assertFalse("score: 1.00" in res.stdout.lower())
   132	
   133	if __name__ == '__main__':
   134	    unittest.main()
